## 2026-04-23 (late-night scheduled_action contract cleanup) ŌĆö explicit `user_reminder` / `assistant_check` create modes replace audience-coercion guessing

### Why this session

Live founder QA after the combined F2 + L1.1 deploy exposed the real remaining scheduled-action bug: unconditional "ą┐ąĮąĖ ą╝ąĄąĮčÅ čćąĄčĆąĄąĘ 2 ą╝ąĖąĮčāčéčŗ" had improved, but honest conditional background requests still degraded. Repro dialogs on `persai-dev` showed the assistant repeatedly promising to "set actionPayload correctly", then failing to do so; the backend silently coerced the malformed `audience="assistant"` create into `audience="user"`, and the eventual push text was the raw condition/instruction (`If in 2 minutes the user has not writtenŌĆ”`) instead of a human reminder. The founder explicitly rejected layering more repair-loop or word-heuristic logic on top ("ą░ ą┐čĆąŠčüčéąŠ ą┐ąŠą┤čāą╝ą░čéčī ąĖ čüą┤ąĄą╗ą░čéčī ą║ą░ą║ ą┐ąŠą╗ąŠąČąĄąĮąŠ ą░ ąĮąĄ ą║ąŠą╗čģąŠąĘąĖčéčī ąĮąĄą╗čīąĘčÅ?") and restated the desired architecture: the tool contract itself must make the two modes explicit, and backend must stop guessing.

### What changed

- **`scheduled_action.create` is now a strict discriminated contract.** The overload on `audience=` is gone from the create path. `packages/runtime-contract/src/index.ts` now defines two create variants: `kind="user_reminder"` (`title` + `reminderText` + exactly one schedule) and `kind="assistant_check"` (`title` + `actionType` + NON-EMPTY `actionPayload` + exactly one schedule). `list` / `pause` / `resume` / `cancel` are unchanged. Storage rows still use `audience="user" | "assistant"` because that remains the right execution-time split in the DB; the distinction is simply no longer model-facing on the create call.
- **Runtime parser/projection switched from `audience` to `kind`.** `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/native-tool-projection.ts:createScheduledActionToolDefinition` now exposes `kind` in the model-visible schema and explains the two modes directly. `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/runtime-scheduled-action-tool.service.ts` rejects legacy `audience=` create calls with `invalid_arguments`, requires `reminderText` for `user_reminder`, requires non-empty `actionPayload` for `assistant_check`, forbids hidden fields on `user_reminder`, and forbids `reminderText` on `assistant_check`. The runtime now sends the strict `kind` shape to the internal API; there is no more "create as assistant and let backend decide if it really meant user".
- **API control-plane no longer guesses or coerces.** `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/control-internal-scheduled-action.service.ts` now parses the same strict `kind` contract. For `kind="user_reminder"` it derives storage `audience="user"` and requires `reminderText`. For `kind="assistant_check"` it derives `audience="assistant"`, requires `actionType`, requires a non-empty `actionPayload`, and rejects `reminderText`. The F2 `looksLikeUnconditionalAssistantTask()` routing-coercion path is removed entirely; `coercedFromAudience` and `scheduled_action_coerced_to_user_push` are no longer part of the happy path.
- **Assistant-side executor prompt now assumes only valid background checks exist.** `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/run-scheduled-assistant-action.service.ts` no longer has a "no payload ŌåÆ convert to user push" fallback branch. Instead, malformed null/empty-payload assistant rows are treated as invalid legacy rows and rejected loudly. Valid assistant runs are instructed to create `kind="user_reminder"` when the condition matches, or another `kind="assistant_check"` with the same payload and a new `runAt` when the condition does not match.
- **Guidance/preset text aligned to the new truth.** `apps/api/prisma/tool-catalog-data.ts` now explains the two explicit create modes and explicitly warns that `reminderText` must be the final human message the user will see, not an instruction dump like "If in 2 minutes...". `apps/api/prisma/bootstrap-preset-data.ts` heartbeat guidance now says to create a `kind="user_reminder"` follow-up when an assistant-side check decides to nudge the user.
- **Tests.** `apps/api/test/control-internal-scheduled-action.service.test.ts` removes the old coercion regression and replaces it with strict contract tests (`assistant_check` requires non-empty payload; `user_reminder` rejects hidden fields). `apps/api/test/run-scheduled-assistant-action.service.test.ts` now expects the executor prompt to speak in `kind="user_reminder"` / `kind="assistant_check"` terms and to reject malformed null-payload rows. `apps/runtime/test/runtime-scheduled-action-tool.service.test.ts` and `apps/runtime/test/turn-execution.service.test.ts` were updated for the new create contract and now verify end-to-end `kind` forwarding.
- **Hidden-run terminal-outcome guard.** `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/send-native-web-chat-turn.service.ts` now forwards runtime `toolInvocations`, and `RunScheduledAssistantActionService` validates the hidden executor's terminal outcome instead of treating every completed turn as success. A background `assistant_check` now succeeds only if it either created a `scheduled_action` reminder or returned a non-empty internal acknowledgement; the previously silent `web_search -> empty completion -> completed` path now throws, logs `scheduled_assistant_action_dispatch_failed` with the tool summary, and falls back into the scheduler retry path instead of disappearing as a fake success.

### Verification gate

- `pnpm --filter @persai/runtime-contract typecheck` clean.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/api typecheck` clean.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/runtime typecheck` clean.

### Live-acceptance (post-deploy on `persai-dev`)

After Argo rolls these out:

1. Ask: `ąØą░ą┐ąŠą╝ąĮąĖ ą╝ąĮąĄ ą┐ąŠąĄčüčéčī čćąĄčĆąĄąĘ ą╝ąĖąĮčāčéčā.` Expect the model to call `scheduled_action.create` with `kind="user_reminder"` and a short final `reminderText`; one minute later the user sees a normal human push (not a technical instruction).
2. Ask: `ąĢčüą╗ąĖ čćąĄčĆąĄąĘ 2 ą╝ąĖąĮčāčéčŗ čÅ ąĮąĄ ąĮą░ą┐ąĖčłčā ą▓ čćą░čé čüą╗ąŠą▓ąŠ "ą║ąŠčüą╝ąŠčü", ą┐ąĖąĮą│ą░ąĮąĖ ą╝ąĄąĮčÅ.` Expect the model to call `scheduled_action.create` with `kind="assistant_check"` plus non-empty `actionPayload`; no immediate coercion to a user reminder; after 2 minutes the hidden check runs and only then, if the word is absent, creates a separate `kind="user_reminder"` push.
3. Ask: `ą¦ąĄčĆąĄąĘ 2 ą╝ąĖąĮčāčéčŗ ą┐čĆąŠą▓ąĄčĆčī ą║čāčĆčü ą┤ąŠą╗ą╗ą░čĆą░ ą║ čĆčāą▒ą╗čÄ, ąĖ ąĄčüą╗ąĖ ąŠąĮ ąĮąĄ čüčéą░ąĮąĄčé ąĮąĖąČąĄ 30 čĆčāą▒ą╗ąĄą╣ ŌĆö ą┐ąĖąĮą│ą░ąĮąĖ ą╝ąĄąĮčÅ.` Expect the same `assistant_check` path. Critically, the delivered user-facing push must NOT be the raw instruction text (`If in 2 minutes...`) because that text no longer belongs in `user_reminder.reminderText`.

### Known follow-ups (out of scope, intentionally)

- No migration/backfill for already-created malformed assistant rows. Old broken rows should be cancelled manually if they still exist; new rows cannot be created in that shape anymore.
- This supersedes the previous F2 claim that "no `kind` discriminator is needed". Repo truth is now the opposite: the explicit discriminator is the clean fix.

## 2026-04-23 (late-evening L1 follow-through) ŌĆö ADR-074 Slice L1.1: cost-counting holes from live observation (conscious revision of L1 founder anchor)

### Why this session

The founder ran the L1 turnkey closeout against `/admin/plans` and immediately hit a cost-counting bypass: `Per-turn cap = 1`, `Loop limit ┬Ę normal = 2`, then ┬½čÅ čüą║ą░ąĘą░ą╗ čüą┤ąĄą╗ą░ą╣ 3 ą║ą░čĆčéąĖąĮą║ąĖ ąĖ ąŠąĮ čüą┤ąĄą╗ą░ą╗, čģąŠčéčÅ per-turn cap=1┬╗. GKE log dive (`gcloud logging read ŌĆ” resource.labels.namespace_name=persai-dev`) confirmed the per-turn cap was firing correctly for actual _invocations_ (`tool-budget-exhausted reason=per_tool_cap limit=1 observed=2`), but the model had passed `image_generate({ count: 3 })` ŌĆö one tool call producing three OpenAI-billed artifacts for the cap-budget price of one. A wider audit found two more holes in the same code path: six cost tools (`tts`, `browser`, `exec`, `shell`, `files`, `scheduled_action`) shipped with **no** code default in `TOOL_HARD_CAP_PER_TURN` so a single turn could fan out to dozens of parallel calls; and the daily-quota counter was a no-op when the plan had `dailyCallLimit: null` while three tools (`memory_write`, `knowledge_search`, `knowledge_fetch`) never called the counter at all, so the founder dashboard was blind to traffic for any tool that the operator had not explicitly capped. Founder directive after I proposed phasing this as A/B: ┬½ą┐ąŠčćąĄą╝čā ąĮąĄą╗čīąĘčÅ čüą┤ąĄą╗ą░čéčī čüčĆą░ąĘčā ąÉ ąÆ ąĖ ąĮąĄ ą┤čĆąŠą▒ąĖčéčī čüąĄčüčüąĖąĖ? ŌĆö ą│ąŠąĮąĖą╝ ą▓čüčæ čĆą░ąĘąŠą╝, čÅ founder, čÅ anchor ąĖ ą╝ąĄąĮčÅą╗┬╗. So this lands as a single L1.1 slice (not stacked into L1) because it consciously revises the original L1 founder anchor that left memory/knowledge tools uncapped ŌĆö the L1.1 spirit preserves that anchor by setting _generous_ defaults, not zero.

### What changed

- **Caps + image_generate schema clamp.** `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/tool-budget-policy.ts:TOOL_HARD_CAP_PER_TURN` ships founder-confirmed defaults for every cost tool (`tts:3`, `browser:3`, `exec:5`, `shell:5`, `files:10`, `scheduled_action:5`, `knowledge_search:5`, `knowledge_fetch:10`, `memory_write:10`). The shared-compaction tools (`summarize_context`, `compact_context`) remain absent because the runtime drives them at most once per turn. `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/native-tool-projection.ts:createImageGenerateToolDefinition` now clamps `count.maximum` to `min(MAX_RUNTIME_IMAGE_GENERATE_COUNT, effectivePerTurnCap)` via the new `resolveImageGenerateCountCap(policy)` helper, so cap=1 mechanically refuses `count > 1` at the model boundary; the description tells the model that each requested image consumes one cap unit and one daily-quota unit.
- **API contract: `units` weight.** `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/domain/workspace-tool-daily-usage.repository.ts` adds an optional `units` parameter (default 1) on both `incrementAndGet` and `consumeWithinLimit`; the Prisma implementation defensively normalises `units` to a positive integer (`normalizeUnits`) and `consumeWithinLimitTx` rejects the _whole_ batch when `currentCount + units > dailyCallLimit` (no partial commits). `track-workspace-quota-usage.service.ts:consumeToolDailyLimit` accepts `units?` and a nullable `dailyCallLimit` ŌĆö when the plan has no daily cap it now falls through to `incrementAndGet` for observability and returns `limit: null`. `consume-internal-runtime-tool-daily-limit.service.ts:parseInput` accepts a nullable `dailyCallLimit` (the runtime forwards what its bundle saw, the API re-resolves the live plan as the source of truth) and a positive-integer `units` (defaults to 1; non-positive integers are rejected with `BadRequestException`).
- **Runtime: always count + missing-quota.** Every cost-tool service drops the prior `if (policy.dailyCallLimit !== null)` guard so it always pings the consume endpoint ŌĆö `runtime-image-generate-tool.service.ts` passes `units: request.count`. The runtime client `persai-internal-api.client.service.ts:consumeToolDailyLimit` widens its type to `dailyCallLimit: number | null` and adds an optional `units?: number`; `ConsumeToolDailyLimitOutcome.allowed=true` now also has `limit: number | null` to model the always-count-no-enforcement branch. `runtime-knowledge-tool.service.ts` adds a small `consumeQuota(bundle, toolCode)` helper that pings the API for both `executeSearchToolCall` and `executeFetchToolCall`; `runtime-memory-write-tool.service.ts:executeToolCall` does the same up front for `memory_write`. Both honour the founder anchor by silently skipping when the assistant has no policy entry for the tool.
- **Admin UI.** `apps/web/app/admin/plans/page.tsx` replaces the misleading "Blank daily cap means this tool inherits the plan default behavior" with the truthful "Blank daily cap = no daily limit (calls are still counted for observability but never refused). Set a positive integer to enforce a hard daily ceiling per workspace; the call is rejected once the counter reaches it." and extends the `Per-turn cap` helptext with the full L1.1 defaults table.
- **Tests.** `apps/runtime/test/tool-budget-policy.test.ts` asserts the L1.1 defaults for every newly-capped tool, keeps the L1 anchor for `compact_context`/`summarize_context` remaining uncapped, and pins `perToolCap("memory_write") === 10` / `perToolCap("knowledge_search") === 5` directly. `apps/api/test/prisma-workspace-tool-daily-usage.repository.test.ts` covers weighted `consumeWithinLimit` (counter jumps by `units`, whole-batch denial when over the cap, defensive normalisation of zero/negative `units`) and weighted `incrementAndGet` (forwards `units`, default `units=1` keeps older callers wire-compatible). `apps/api/test/consume-internal-runtime-tool-daily-limit.service.test.ts` adds: parsing accepts an absent `units` (treated as 1) and a null `dailyCallLimit`; `image_generate`-style weighted call (`units=4`) is forwarded verbatim; null-cap plan still succeeds and returns `limit: null` (always-count); deactivated tool remains a hard rejection (only blocking path left); `units=0` and negative values are rejected with `BadRequestException`. The runtime fakes in `runtime-tts-tool.service.test.ts`, `runtime-video-generate-tool.service.test.ts`, `turn-execution.service.test.ts`, `runtime-knowledge-tool.service.test.ts`, `runtime-memory-write-tool.service.test.ts`, and `runtime-scheduled-action-tool.service.test.ts` widen their mock signatures and add `consumeToolDailyLimit` mocks where they were previously absent.

### Verification gate

- `pnpm --filter @persai/api typecheck` / `lint` / `test` clean.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/runtime typecheck` / `lint` / `test` clean.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/web typecheck` / `lint` / `test` clean.
- `pnpm format:check` clean across touched files.

### Live-acceptance (post-deploy on `persai-dev`)

After Argo rolls these out:

1. Ask the assistant for ┬½čüą│ąĄąĮąĄčĆąĖčĆčāą╣ 4 ą║ą░čĆčéąĖąĮą║ąĖ┬╗ on an assistant where `image_generate.dailyCallLimit = 50` and `perTurnCap = 4`. Expect: one provider call returning 4 artifacts; `tool_daily_usage.callCount` advances by 4 (not 1) for the assistant's workspace; `quota_status` reports `image_generate.currentCount += 4`.
2. Ask the assistant for ┬½čüą│ąĄąĮąĄčĆąĖčĆčāą╣ 3 ą║ą░čĆčéąĖąĮą║ąĖ┬╗ on an assistant where `perTurnCap = 1`. Expect: the model literally cannot pass `count > 1` (the schema clamp is enforced at the model boundary); the model emits three sequential `image_generate({ count: 1 })` calls and the third one returns `tool_budget_exhausted reason=per_tool_cap limit=1 observed=2`.
3. Ask the assistant for ┬½ąŠąĘą▓čāčćčī 5 ą║ąŠčĆąŠčéą║ąĖčģ čäčĆą░ąĘ ą┐ąŠą┤čĆčÅą┤┬╗ on an assistant where `tts` has no daily cap configured. Expect: the model fans out at most 3 parallel `tts` calls (`per_tool_cap` fires on the 4th), and each successful call advances `tool_daily_usage` by 1 even though `dailyCallLimit` is null on the plan.
4. Trigger `memory_write` and `knowledge_search` on any assistant. Expect: both tools appear in `tool_daily_usage` after one turn (previously they were invisible to the counter).

### Known follow-ups (out of scope, intentionally)

- R1 plurality detection / `requestedBudget`, R2 true wall-clock parallelism, R3 compound tools all remain explicitly out of scope.
- This slice does not change cap _values_ on any existing plan ŌĆö operators keep whatever daily caps they already configured; L1.1 only fixes the _measurement_ layer so caps and counters now match what providers actually bill.
- The ADR-074 ┬¦ L1.1 section explicitly flags the memory/knowledge cap defaults as a conscious revision of the original L1 founder anchor, so a future reader does not misread it as silent.

## 2026-04-23 (post-F1 same-night) ŌĆö Background scheduled-action stack v2: empty-completion safety + audience routing-coercion + executor-style assistant prompt + duplicate-guard removal (second FIX bundle stacked on F1, still no new ADR slice)

### Why this session

After the F1 bundle landed on `persai-dev`, the founder reproduced the original bug again: ┬½ąÆčĆąĄą╝čÅ ą▓čŗčłą╗ąŠ ąĮąĖčćąĄą│ąŠ ąĮąĄ ą┐čĆąŠąĖąĘąŠčłą╗ąŠ ąĖą┤ąĖ ąĖčēąĖ ą┐čĆąĖčćąĖąĮčā ą▓ log ąĖ čāčüčéčĆą░ąĮčÅą╣ ąĮą░ą┤ąŠąĄą╗ąŠ čāąČąĄ┬╗. Tracing taskId `fc23766c-ŌĆ”` between `2026-04-23T11:56:55Z` and `2026-04-23T12:04:39Z` showed the new structured retry/dead-letter contract worked exactly as designed ŌĆö but it was retrying real `Provider gateway request failed with status 500.` errors, attempt 1 ŌåÆ 2 ŌåÆ 3 ŌåÆ 4 ŌåÆ 5 ŌåÆ `task_disabled_after_exhausted_retries`. OpenAI logs (UI screenshots from the founder) showed the model legitimately calling `scheduled_action({"action":"list", "audience":"assistant"})` and then producing **no output** ŌĆö no text, no tool call. The founder's directive was unambiguous: ┬½ąØąĄ ąĮčāąČąĮąŠ ąĮąĖą║ą░ą║ąĖčģ ąĮą░čĆąĄąĘąŠą║ f2 f3 f10 ąĮą░čģąĄčĆą░ ą┤ąĄą╗ą░ą╣ čĆą░ąĘąŠą╝┬╗. So the F2 / F3 / prompt rewrite all land in one FIX bundle here, again no new ADR slice.

### What changed

- **(F2-a) Provider gateway no longer treats empty completions as 500.** `apps/provider-gateway/src/modules/providers/openai/openai-provider.client.ts:generateText` and `streamText` now return `text: null, stopReason: "completed", toolCalls: []` and emit a structured `openai_empty_completion` warn (with `outputItemTypes`, `outputItemCount`, `hasOutputText`, `finishReason`, `inputTokens`, `outputTokens`, `runtimeRequestId`, `transport`) for the "no text + no tool calls" shape, instead of throwing / yielding `failed`. Same fix in `apps/provider-gateway/src/modules/providers/anthropic/anthropic-provider.client.ts` with `anthropic_empty_completion`. The `provider_invalid_response` failed event is preserved for genuinely broken shapes (e.g. `stop_reason="tool_use"` with zero tool calls). New helper functions: `summarizeOutputItemTypes` / `readNonStreamingFinishReason` / `readMetadataRequestId` (OpenAI) and `summarizeAnthropicContentBlockTypes` (Anthropic).
- **(F2-b) Runtime tolerates empty turns.** `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/turn-execution.service.ts:buildTurnResult` no longer throws `InternalServerErrorException` on `providerResult.text === null`. It materialises `assistantText: ""` (chat surfaces simply do not render an empty bubble) and emits one warn line so we can spot pathological patterns without breaking flow.
- **(F2-c) Audience routing-coercion in `ControlInternalScheduledActionService.createScheduledAction`.** Conservative heuristic `looksLikeUnconditionalAssistantTask({ actionPayload? })` returns true exactly when `actionPayload` is `undefined` or has zero keys. When it matches an `audience="assistant"` create, the row is transparently rewritten to `audience="user"` (with `reminderText` resolved as `actionPayload?.reminderText ?? input.reminderText ?? input.title`, `actionType` / `actionPayload` dropped), the user-side telegram reminder target is persisted, and the response carries `coercedFromAudience: "assistant"`. The runtime tool service surfaces a `warning` on the model-visible result (`Routed to audience="user" because actionPayload was empty. For unconditional reminders, call scheduled_action with audience="user" directly.`) so the model self-corrects on the next turn. A structured `scheduled_action_coerced_to_user_push` log line records every coercion. Rows _with_ a non-empty `actionPayload` are left untouched.
- **(F2-d) `buildScheduledActionPrompt` rewritten as an EXECUTOR brief.** Two branches keyed on whether `actionPayload` is non-empty. WITH-payload turns must end with exactly one `scheduled_action(action="create", audience="user", delayMs=1, ŌĆ”)` (condition met) OR `scheduled_action(action="create", audience="assistant", actionType=<same>, actionPayload=<same>, runAt=<next-check>)` (condition not met). WITHOUT-payload turns (which should not normally be reached because of F2-c) must immediately create an `audience="user"` push so the user receives something. Both branches forbid `scheduled_action(action="list")` (the actionPayload IS the brief) and forbid mirror `audience="assistant"` rows without changing `runAt`. The header explicitly says `Silence is not a valid outcome.` The old "decider" framing with `Prefer silence over guessing` is gone for this surface only ŌĆö chat-side proactive prompts elsewhere are unchanged.
- **(F2-e) F1 `duplicate_audience_create_detected` guard removed.** Once F2-c routing-coercion lands, every "ą┐ąĮąĖ ą╝ąĄąĮčÅ čćąĄčĆąĄąĘ X" turn legitimately creates exactly one `audience="user"` row from a colloquial `audience="assistant"` request, and the F1 warn would have fired on every healthy coercion. `warnIfDuplicateAudienceCreate`, `DUPLICATE_AUDIENCE_CREATE_WINDOW_MS`, `normalizeTitleForDuplicateCheck` are gone. The `Nested assistant scheduled_action creation is not allowed` guard against recursion inside an already-running assistant-side check turn is preserved.
- **(F2-f) `scheduled_action.modelUsageGuidance` rewritten** in `apps/api/prisma/tool-catalog-data.ts`. Pick `audience="user"` for unconditional pings (the user WILL receive them); pick `audience="assistant"` only for conditional rechecks where you provide a non-empty `actionPayload` describing what to evaluate; ignore the colloquial "ą┐ąŠčüčéą░ą▓čī čüąĄą▒ąĄ" phrasing ŌĆö pick by SEMANTICS not wording. The text also tells the model that an empty `actionPayload` will be coerced server-side, so the model has a path to correct itself.
- **Tests.** New `apps/provider-gateway/test/openai-empty-completion.test.ts` and `apps/provider-gateway/test/anthropic-empty-completion.test.ts` each pin three scenarios (empty non-streaming ŌåÆ text=null, tool_calls still surface, empty streaming ŌåÆ single `completed` event with text=null + no `failed` event). Both wired into `apps/provider-gateway/test/run-suite.ts` (also closes a latent miss ŌĆö `runOpenAIEmptyCompletionTest` was imported but never called). New `runUnconditionalAssistantCoercedToUserTest` in `apps/api/test/control-internal-scheduled-action.service.test.ts` asserts a `audience="assistant"` create with no `actionPayload` is rewritten to `audience="user"` (DB row, telegram reminder target persisted, `coercedFromAudience: "assistant"` in response). `apps/api/test/run-scheduled-assistant-action.service.test.ts` rewritten to pin the new executor-style prompt shape (with-payload branch and no-payload branch).

### Verification gate

- `pnpm --filter @persai/api typecheck` / `lint` / `test` clean.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/provider-gateway typecheck` / `lint` / `test` clean.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/runtime typecheck` / `lint` / `test` clean.

### Live-acceptance (post-deploy on `persai-dev`)

After Argo rolls these out:

1. Ask the assistant ┬½ą¤ąŠčüčéą░ą▓čī čüąĄą▒ąĄ ąĘą░ą┤ą░čćčā ą┐ąĮčāčéčī ą╝ąĄąĮčÅ čćąĄčĆąĄąĘ 2 ą╝ąĖąĮčāčéčŗ, čćčéąŠą▒čŗ čéą▓ąŠčÅ čäąŠąĮąŠą▓ą░čÅ ąĘą░ą┤ą░čćą░ čüčĆą░ą▒ąŠčéą░ą╗ą░, ą░ čéčŗ ą╝ąĄąĮčÅ push, čŹčéąŠ čéąĄčüčé┬╗ ŌĆö expect:
   - In `api` pod logs, exactly one `scheduled_action_coerced_to_user_push` line (with the title and `originalActionType`).
   - The `assistant_task_registry_items` row stored as `audience="user"`, `actionType=null`.
   - 2 minutes later, exactly one push delivered to the user (telegram + web depending on bound channel).
   - No `Provider gateway request failed with status 500.` lines for the task's `externalRef`.
2. If the model still creates an `audience="assistant"` row WITH a non-empty `actionPayload` (e.g. for a real conditional probe like "watch FX rate, ping me if it goes above 100"), the F2-d prompt fires and the assistant-side check turn is expected to end with exactly one `scheduled_action.create`. Empty completions on that turn now surface as `openai_empty_completion` / `anthropic_empty_completion` warns instead of cascading into 500s, so a single rare model glitch no longer dead-letters the whole task.
3. The previously dead-lettered tasks (e.g. `fc23766c-ŌĆ”`) will be cleaned up manually by the founder per in-session decision; no migration script ships in this bundle.

### Known follow-ups (out of scope, intentionally)

- No `kind` / `executionMode` discriminator added to the `scheduled_action` tool input schema ŌĆö the simpler "empty actionPayload ŌåÆ coerce" heuristic plus the model-visible `warning` is enough to fix the observed live failure mode without forcing every caller to learn a new schema field.
- ADR-074 was deliberately not touched (founder's call). If a future reviewer wants this in the ADR archaeology, it lives entirely in this CHANGELOG + SESSION-HANDOFF entry, stacked on the F1 entry below.

## 2026-04-23 (post-T2 / post-L1 night) ŌĆö Background scheduled-action stack: fail-soft observability + presence-baseline UUID crash + unified retry/dead-letter contract + anti-duplicate prompt guidance (single FIX bundle, no new ADR slice)

### Why this session

Right after T1 / T2 closed live on `persai-dev`, the founder hit a multi-symptom regression in the proactive-push stack: ┬½ą¦čéąŠ čéąŠ čüą╗ąŠą╝ą░ą╗ąŠčüčī ŌĆö čäąŠąĮąŠą▓čŗąĄ ąĘą░ą┤ą░čćąĖ ąĮąĄ čüčĆą░ą▒ą░čéčŗą▓ą░čÄčé ąĖ ą░čüąĖčüčéąĄąĮčé ąĮąĄ push ąĖ ąĖąĮąŠą│ą┤ą░ ą▓ą╝ąĄčüčéąŠ ąŠą┤ąĮąŠą╣ ą┤ąĄą╗ą░ąĄčé čüčĆą░ąĘčā 2, ąŠą┤ąĮčā ą┤ą╗čÅ čüąĄą▒čÅ ąŠą┤ąĮčā ą┤ą╗čÅ user, ąĖ čćą░čüčéčī, ą║ąŠčéąŠčĆą░čÅ ąĘą░ čäąŠąĮąŠą▓čŗąĄ ąĘą░ą┤ą░čćąĖ ąŠčéą▓ąĄčćą░ąĄčé, ąŠčćąĄąĮčī čüčŗčĆą░čÅ ŌĆö ą┐ąŠčüą╝ąŠčéčĆąĖ ą╗ąŠą│ąĖ ą┤ą╗čÅ ąĮą░čćą░ą╗ą░┬╗ and a follow-up ┬½`d7277de7` ą┤čæčĆą│ą░ą╗čüčÅ 3 čĆą░ąĘą░ ą┐ąŠą┤čĆčÅą┤ ąĘą░ ą╝ąĖąĮčāčéčā ąĖ ąĘą░čéąĖčģ, ą▒ąĄąĘ ą║ą░ą║ąŠą│ąŠ-ą╗ąĖą▒ąŠ čüąĖą│ąĮą░ą╗ą░ ą┐ąŠą╗čīąĘąŠą▓ą░čéąĄą╗čÄ ŌĆö ą┐ąŠčćąĄą╝čā?┬╗. Initial GKE log dive across `api` / `provider-gateway` / `runtime` pods turned up four interlocking issues; the founder's explicit decision was ┬½čŹčéąŠ ąĮąĄ ą┐čĆčÅą╝ ąŠą│čĆąŠą╝ąĮčŗąĄ ą░čĆčģąĖčéąĄą║čéčāčĆąĮčŗąĄ ą┐čĆą░ą▓ą║ąĖ ŌĆ” L1 čāąČąĄ ą│ąŠčéąŠą▓, ą┐čĆąĄą┤ą╗ą░ą│ą░čÄ čüąĄą╣čćą░čü ąĖčüą┐čĆą░ą▓ąĖčéčī ą▓čüčæ ąĖ ąĮąĄ ą▓ą┐ąĖčüčŗą▓ą░čéčī ą▓ ADR, ą░ ąĘą░ą┐ąĖčüą░čéčī čéąŠą╗čīą║ąŠ ą▓ ąĖčüčéąŠčĆąĖčÄ ą┐čĆą░ą▓ąŠą║, ą┐ąŠčéąŠą╝čā čćčéąŠ čŹčéąŠ ą┐ąŠ čćą░čüčéąĖ FIX ą░ ąĮąĄ ą░čĆčģąĖčéąĄą║čéčāčĆą░ čüąĄčĆčīčæąĘąĮą░čÅ┬╗. So this lands as one FIX bundle in CHANGELOG + SESSION-HANDOFF only ŌĆö no new ADR-074 slice, no Status block edits.

### What changed

- **Provider-gateway / api / runtime exception logging ŌĆö `msg:{}` ŌåÆ structured `err`.** Nest's default `ExceptionsHandler` invokes `LoggerService.error(error, stack, context)` with the raw `Error` object as `message`; every `AppLoggerService` in the repo typed `message: string` and routed the value into pino's mergingObject arg, where Error instances serialise to `{}`. New shared helper `normalizeErrorLogPayload(message, trace)` in `packages/logger/src/index.ts` extracts `name` / `message` / `stack` / `cause` (with `cause` itself unwrapped if it's an Error). All three `AppLoggerService` implementations (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `apps/provider-gateway`) widen `error()` / `fatal()` to `unknown` and route through the helper. Strings + traces continue to log byte-for-byte as before; the structured `err` field appears whenever Nest hands us an Error.
- **`PersaiScheduledActionSchedulerService` ŌĆö unstructured strings ŌåÆ structured events.** Every failure decision in `processClaimedTask`'s catch branch now emits `scheduled_action_failed_deferred` / `scheduled_action_failed_advanced_to_next_recurring_run` / `task_disabled_after_exhausted_retries` with `{ taskId, externalRef, audience, scheduleKind, attemptCount, decision, errorName, errorMessage, ŌĆ”backoffMs }` so we can pivot any of those fields in `gcloud logging read` without parsing a sentence. Verified live in unit-test stdout (each event prints with all keys present).
- **T1 presence-baseline lookup ŌĆö silent UUID crash on every scheduled-action turn.** `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/turn-context-hydration.service.ts:computePresenceBlock` was passing `id: { not: input.idempotencyKey }` straight into `prisma.assistantChatMessage.findFirst`, but `AssistantChatMessage.id` is a Postgres `uuid` column and `RuntimeTurnRequest.idempotencyKey` is a free-form string (scheduled actions inject `"scheduled-action:<externalRef>:<runAtMs>"`). Postgres rejected with `Inconsistent column data: Error creating UUID, expected ŌĆ”, found 's' at 1`, the existing `try/catch` swallowed the error, and presence rendered with **null timestamps** every time ŌĆö fully defeating T1 sense-of-time on the very codepath (assistant-side proactive runs) that needs it most. New exported predicate `isUuidLikeIdempotencyKey(value)` (strict 8-4-4-4-12 hex regex) gates the exclusion: scheduled-action turns skip the clause entirely; legitimate web-chat turns where `idempotencyKey` is a UUID continue to exclude the just-recorded inbound message exactly as before.
- **Retry / lifecycle hygiene ŌĆö unified contract for both audiences (`audience="assistant"` AND `audience="user"`).** `audience="assistant"` was disabling silently after 5 receipt-rows by counting `RuntimeTurnReceipt.where(externalThreadKey, status in ("failed","interrupted"))`; that counter saturated within ~1.5 wall-clock minutes because a single failing turn produced multiple receipt rows (one per native tool-loop iteration). Hence `d7277de7 ą┤čæčĆą│ą░ą╗čüčÅ 3 čĆą░ąĘą░ ą┐ąŠą┤čĆčÅą┤ ąĘą░ ą╝ąĖąĮčāčéčā ąĖ ąĘą░čéąĖčģ` ŌĆö disabled with **zero** observable signal. `audience="user"` had no cap at all and kept re-firing every flat 30 s forever. New column `attempt_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0` plus `last_error_message VARCHAR(2000)` and `last_error_at TIMESTAMPTZ(6)` on `assistant_task_registry_items` via reversible additive migration `apps/api/prisma/migrations/20260423040000_adr074_f1_scheduler_attempt_tracking/migration.sql`. The catch branch now bumps `attemptCount` exactly once per `processClaimedTask` invocation that ends in failure-and-retry, defers with exponential backoff `min(30s ┬Ę 2^(attempt-1), 1h)` (new exported helper `computeRetryBackoffMs`), and disables the task after `MAX_ATTEMPTS = 5` for **both** audiences ŌĆö emitting `task_disabled_after_exhausted_retries` with the truncated last-error message. Recurring `audience="assistant"` tasks (`schedule.kind !== "at"`) still **advance to next slot** on a non-still-processing failure (so a failing daily probe doesn't get permanently disabled). Success in `completeUserActionRun` / `completeAssistantActionRun` resets `attemptCount = 0`, `lastErrorMessage = null`, `lastErrorAt = null`. The dead `countFailedAssistantActionReceipts` helper and the old `ASSISTANT_SCHEDULED_ACTION_MAX_FAILED_RECEIPTS` / `SCHEDULED_ACTION_RETRY_DELAY_MS` constants were removed.
- **Anti-duplicate prompt guidance + soft diagnostic warn.** `scheduled_action.modelUsageGuidance` (`apps/api/prisma/tool-catalog-data.ts`) and rule 6 of `buildScheduledActionPrompt` (`run-scheduled-assistant-action.service.ts`) now explicitly forbid creating both `audience="user"` AND `audience="assistant"` for the same intent in a single turn ŌĆö the correct pattern is "one `audience="user"` for an explicit user-facing nudge", or "one `audience="assistant"` that, when it later fires, conditionally creates one `audience="user"` follow-up with `delayMs=1`". `ControlInternalScheduledActionService.createScheduledAction` additionally emits a soft `duplicate_audience_create_detected` warn with `{ assistantId, newTaskId, newTaskAudience, siblingTaskId, siblingAudience, normalizedTitle }` when the new task's normalized title matches a task with the opposite audience created within the last 60 s by the same assistant ŌĆö diagnostic only, the row is still created so a legitimate same-title-but-distinct edge case never silently breaks.
- **Tests.** New focused regressions: `apps/api/test/scheduled-action-retry-backoff.test.ts` pins the exponential curve + 1-h ceiling + floor-to-1 normalisation. `apps/api/test/normalize-error-log-payload.test.ts` covers Error / Error-with-cause / string + trace / object / circular-object cases. `apps/runtime/test/presence-baseline-uuid-guard.test.ts` pins rejection of `scheduled-action:<ŌĆ”>` shapes, almost-UUIDs, null/undefined, empty strings, and acceptance of canonical 8-4-4-4-12 (case-insensitive). Existing `apps/api/test/persai-scheduled-action-scheduler.service.test.ts` was migrated off the dead `failedReceiptsByExternalRef` plumbing ŌĆö `runAssistantFailureExhaustionTest` now seeds `attemptCount: 4` so the next bump trips `MAX_ATTEMPTS = 5`, and the recurring-cap test confirms a recurring task at `attemptCount: 4` still advances rather than disabling.

### Verification gate

- `pnpm --filter @persai/api typecheck` clean.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/api lint` clean.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/runtime typecheck` / `lint` clean.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/provider-gateway typecheck` / `lint` clean.
- `pnpm format:check` clean across the whole repo.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/persai-scheduled-action-scheduler.service.test.ts` green (with all new structured `task_disabled_after_exhausted_retries` / `scheduled_action_failed_deferred` events visible in stdout).
- `pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/scheduled-action-retry-backoff.test.ts` green.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/normalize-error-log-payload.test.ts` green.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/control-internal-scheduled-action.service.test.ts` green.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/runtime exec tsx test/presence-baseline-uuid-guard.test.ts` green.

### Live-acceptance (post-deploy on `persai-dev`)

After Argo rolls these out:

1. Trigger an `audience="assistant"` scheduled action that we know fails upstream (e.g. provider-gateway 500). In GKE logs, expect:
   - `provider-gateway` ExceptionsHandler now logs `{level:50, err: { name, message, stack }}` instead of `msg:{}`.
   - `PersaiScheduledActionSchedulerService` logs `scheduled_action_failed_deferred` with `attemptCount: 1`, `backoffMs: 30000`, and structured `errorName` / `errorMessage`.
   - On the 5th attempt, exactly one `task_disabled_after_exhausted_retries` line appears with `attemptCount: 5`.
2. Trigger a scheduled-action turn on a thread with prior user messages. Expect zero `Presence baseline lookup failed; rendering with null timestamps` warnings, and the model's prompt to include the populated `time_since_last_user_message_*` fields.
3. Ask the assistant ┬½ą¤ąŠčüčéą░ą▓čī čüąĄą▒ąĄ ąĘą░ą┤ą░čćčā ą┐ąĮčāčéčī ą╝ąĄąĮčÅ čćąĄčĆąĄąĘ 2 ą╝ąĖąĮčāčéčŗ┬╗ ŌĆö expect exactly **one** row in `assistant_task_registry_items` for that intent (one `audience="user"`, no shadow `audience="assistant"`). If the model regresses, expect a `duplicate_audience_create_detected` warn line in `api` pod logs but the rows still created.

### Known follow-ups (out of scope, intentionally)

- Admin task-list UI surfacing `lastErrorMessage` as a "failed" pill ŌĆö column is populated and visible in admin DB inspection now, UI deferred to a separate small fix.
- ADR-074 was deliberately not touched (founder's call). If a future reviewer wants this in the ADR archaeology, it lives entirely in this CHANGELOG + SESSION-HANDOFF entry.

## 2026-04-23 (post-L1 evening) ŌĆö Web chat slow-mo cadence watchdog no longer false-fires on long tool calls (founder repro on `persai-dev` while asking for `image_generate`; confirmed by GKE logs)

### Why this session

Right after the L1 turnkey closeout deployed to `persai-dev`, the founder hit the red `Streaming ended before a full answer was completed.` banner under the chat composer when asking the assistant for two image-generate results. The founder explicitly framed it as "ąĮąĄ čüą▓čÅąĘą░ąĮą░ čü L1, čŹčéąŠ čüčéą░čĆą░čÅ ąĖąĘ-ąĘą░ fix čüą╗ąŠčāą╝ąŠ webchat ŌĆö ą┐čĆąĖ ą▓čŗąĘąŠą▓ąĄ ą┤ą╗ąĖąĮąĮčŗčģ tool ą╗ąŠąČąĮąŠ čüčĆą░ą▒ą░čéčŗą▓ą░ąĄčé, ą┤ąŠą╗ąČąĮą░ čüčĆą░ą▒ą░čéčŗą▓ą░čéčī čéąŠą╗čīą║ąŠ ąĮą░ čéąĄą║čüčé." This session captured the exact upstream chain in GKE logs, located the false-positive trigger in the cadence watchdog, and shipped the minimal fix.

### What changed

- **Diagnosis (one-line summary).** The cadence watchdog's `silent` timer (default 8 s) was being reset by `tool_started` (via `recordActivity`) and then ticking through the tool's actual execution span. Long tools (`image_generate`, `video_generate`, slow `web_fetch`) routinely take 15ŌĆō60 s with no intermediate chunks, so at ~`silentMs` the watchdog fired, `internalAbort.abort()` killed the runtime fetch, the API returned `failed/runtime_invalid_response` ("Runtime stream finished without assistant output."), and the web client classifier matched the `"stream"` substring and surfaced the bilingual banner. GKE evidence: `web_stream_stall_detected reason=silent silentMs=8000 rollingAvgMs=n/a rollingWindow=n/a observedGaps=0 accumulatedLen=0` ~11 s after `web_runtime_route` for an `image_generate`-driven turn (zero text-delta gaps observed, zero accumulated text ŌĆö purely a tool-execution gap that was misclassified as a text stall).
- **Watchdog (`apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/cadence-watchdog.ts`).** Two new methods on the `CadenceWatchdog` interface: `recordToolStarted()` and `recordToolFinished()`. They maintain an internal `inflightToolCount` counter; while the counter is `> 0`, `startSilentTimer()` short-circuits so the silent timer is **suspended** for the union of all in-flight tool spans. `recordToolFinished()` re-arms the timer with `lastDeltaAtMs = now()` once the **last** tool resolves. Both methods also move the inter-delta anchor forward (mirroring the existing `recordActivity` semantics) so resumed text deltas after the tool finishes are not penalized by the tool's execution span polluting the `slow_avg` rolling window. `recordToolFinished()` tolerates over-decrement defensively (extra calls are no-ops, the counter never goes negative) so a duplicate end event from upstream demuxing cannot indefinitely keep the suspension active. The file header comment was extended with a "Tool-inflight suspension" paragraph that documents the design intent ("the watchdog is meant to detect slow TEXT streaming, not slow tool execution; per-tool execution timeouts live elsewhere ŌĆö provider gateway / runtime").
- **Web-chat consumer (`apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/stream-web-chat-turn.service.ts`).** On `chunk.type === "tool"`, the consumer now calls `watchdog.recordToolStarted()` for `toolPhase === "start"` and `watchdog.recordToolFinished()` for `toolPhase === "end"` instead of the generic `watchdog.recordActivity()`. `chunk.type === "thinking" / "media" / "done"` continue to use `recordActivity()` because those are genuine in-flight progress signals during the text phase. The change is local to the webchat consumer; the Telegram consumer (`send-native-telegram-turn.service.ts`) does not use the cadence watchdog so there is no parallel change there.
- **Tests (`apps/api/test/cadence-watchdog.test.ts`).** Five new unit tests with the existing `FakeClock` harness (the suite grows from 14 to 19, all green): (1) `recordToolStarted` suspends the silent timer for the entire tool span (60 s tool with `silentMs = 5000` does not fire, then `recordToolFinished` re-arms and fires `silentMs` later); (2) `recordToolStarted` clears an already-armed silent timer (healthy text streaming ŌåÆ tool starts mid-stream ŌåÆ timer is suspended even if it was previously armed by `recordDelta`); (3) overlapping tools (counter `0ŌåÆ1ŌåÆ2` then `2ŌåÆ1ŌåÆ0`) keep the timer suspended until the **last** finish; (4) over-decrement is tolerated (extra `recordToolFinished` calls do not push the counter negative or break a subsequent genuine tool span); (5) tool spans do not pollute `slow_avg` of resumed text deltas (45 s tool span between two healthy text bursts does not flag `slow_avg`).

### Verification gate

- `pnpm --filter @persai/api typecheck` clean.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/api lint` clean (touched files have zero lints).
- `pnpm exec prettier --check apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/cadence-watchdog.ts apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/stream-web-chat-turn.service.ts apps/api/test/cadence-watchdog.test.ts` clean. (The repo-wide `pnpm format:check` reports a pre-existing format issue in `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/turn-context-hydration.service.ts` that belongs to an unrelated in-flight scheduler workstream and is not part of this fix.)
- `pnpm --filter @persai/api test` clean (full suite green).
- `pnpm --filter @persai/api exec node --import tsx --test test/cadence-watchdog.test.ts` reports `# tests 19 # pass 19 # fail 0` (5 new + 14 existing).
- `pnpm --filter @persai/runtime test` clean (custom suite via `tsx test/run-suite.ts`, exit 0; no runtime code was touched but the run reconfirms no contract regression).

### Out of scope

- The classifier mapping in `apps/web/app/app/assistant-api-client.ts:666` that maps any error message containing the substring `"stream"` to `stream_incomplete` is intentionally **left as-is** ŌĆö it is the correct catch-all for genuine stream-incomplete cases; the fix removes the **false** trigger source instead of papering over it on the client.
- Per-tool execution timeouts and circuit-breakers (already enforced in the provider gateway / runtime) are not touched.
- No bundle / Prisma / OpenAPI changes; no new env / config; no admin UI surface.

### Awaiting

- Founder push to remote (the user explicitly said "ąöąĄą╗ą░ą╣ ąĮąŠ ąĮąĄ push čÅ čüą║ą░ąČčā ą┐ąŠčéąŠą╝ ą║ąŠą│ą┤ą░" ŌĆö do not push without an explicit go-ahead). Once pushed and rolled out via Argo CD, the next `image_generate` / `video_generate` request on `persai-dev` should complete cleanly with no `Streaming endedŌĆ”` banner; GKE logs should no longer record `web_stream_stall_detected reason=silent` lines tied to long tool spans (only genuine slow-text stalls).

---

## 2026-04-23 (very late evening) ŌĆö ADR-074 Slice L1 turnkey closeout ŌĆö API-side bundle compile + admin PATCH + admin UI for the L1 tool-loop budgets (founder Q9-C closeout; code-landed; verification gate green)

### Why this session

Right after the L1 follow-up (next entry below) shipped the runtime read for `runtime.toolBudgets` and `RuntimeToolPolicy.perTurnCap`, the founder said: **┬½ą¦čéąŠ ąØąĢ čüą┤ąĄą╗ą░ąĮąŠ - čéą░ą║ ą┤ąĄą╗ą░ą╣ ą┤ąŠ čāą╝ą░ čāąČąĄ ą▓čüąĄ ą┐ąŠą┤ ą║ą╗čÄčć┬╗**. The previous slice deliberately stopped at the runtime side and left the API-side compile pipeline + admin UI for "a follow-up". This closeout wires the API end-to-end so the founder can edit the per-mode loop limits and per-tool hard caps from `/admin/plans` and have them flow through `MaterializeAssistantPublishedVersionService` into every freshly-published bundle without a runtime release.

### What changed

- **Schema.** New nullable `Int? per_turn_cap` column on `plan_catalog_tool_activations` via reversible additive migration `apps/api/prisma/migrations/20260423030000_adr074_l1_plan_per_turn_cap/migration.sql` (NULL = inherit code default; `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER` = uncap on this plan). The per-mode loop-limit overrides reuse the existing `PlanCatalogPlan.billingProviderHints` JSON column under a versioned `toolBudgets` block (`{ schema: "persai.toolBudgets.v1", loopLimitByMode: { normal, premium, reasoning } }`) ŌĆö no new column for them.
- **Domain + repo.** `AssistantPlanCatalogToolActivation` (domain) and `AssistantPlanCatalogToolActivationOverride` (repo write input) gain a non-optional `perTurnCap: number | null` field; `PrismaAssistantPlanCatalogRepository.mapToDomain` and `syncToolActivationsForPlan` carry the column through the upsert.
- **API parser / serializer.** New helper `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/tool-budgets-policy.ts` with `createDefaultPlanToolBudgets` / `parsePlanToolBudgets` (strict ŌĆö `BadRequestException` on 0 / -1 / fractional / non-numeric per leaf with the right field path) / `resolveStoredPlanToolBudgets` (lenient ŌĆö garbage degrades to NULL so an old/garbage row never blocks compile) / `toPlanToolBudgetsDocument` (emits the `persai.toolBudgets.v1` schema marker) / `hasAnyToolBudgetOverride` (used to skip the JSON block when every leaf is NULL).
- **Admin DTO.** `AdminPlanToolActivation`, `AdminPlanToolActivationInput`, `AdminPlanInput`, `AdminPlanState` carry `perTurnCap` and `toolBudgets`. `ManageAdminPlansService.parsePlanInput` reads `parsed.toolBudgets`; `parseToolActivations` validates `typed.perTurnCap`; `toWriteInput` only emits the `toolBudgets` JSON block when there is at least one override (compact bundles for the common case); `toCanonicalToolActivationOverrides` propagates `perTurnCap` into the repo write; `toAdminPlanState` round-trips both fields back via `resolveStoredPlanToolBudgets`.
- **Bundle compile.** `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/runtime-tool-policy.ts:ToolQuotaPolicyEntry` gains `perTurnCap: number | null`; `resolveRuntimeToolPolicies` builds a `perTurnCapByCode` map mirroring `dailyLimitByCode` and stamps the value onto every `RuntimeToolPolicy` it constructs (synthetic system tools and hidden-internal tools always emit `perTurnCap: null`). `MaterializeAssistantPublishedVersionService.resolveToolQuotaPolicy` now selects `perTurnCap` from `prisma.planCatalogToolActivation` and forwards it; new private `resolvePlanToolBudgets(planCode)` reads `PlanCatalogPlan.billingProviderHints.toolBudgets.loopLimitByMode`, sanitizes each leaf (positive integers only), and `buildRuntimeArtifacts` only injects `runtime.toolBudgets: { loopLimitByMode }` into the compile input when at least one leaf is non-null (`hasAnyLoopOverride`).
- **Warm-path validation.** `apps/runtime/src/modules/bundles/runtime-bundle-registry.service.ts:assertValidToolPolicy` rejects `perTurnCap` that is neither null nor a strictly-positive integer; new `assertToolBudgetsConfig` rejects malformed `runtime.toolBudgets.loopLimitByMode.{normal,premium,reasoning}` with the same rule. So a misconfigured override aborts at warm-path-load instead of silently falling back to the code default.
- **Seeding.** `apps/api/prisma/tool-catalog-data.ts:STARTER_TRIAL_TOOL_POLICY` gains an explicit `perTurnCap: number | null` field on every entry (every starter row is `null` so the seeded plan inherits the runtime defaults); `apps/api/prisma/seed.ts` and `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/seed-tool-catalog.service.ts:syncToolActivations` carry the column through their respective `planCatalogToolActivation.upsert` calls.
- **Public API contract.** `packages/contracts/openapi.yaml` adds `perTurnCap` (nullable integer with prose) to `AdminPlanToolActivation` / `AdminPlanToolActivationInput` and a new `AdminPlanToolBudgets` schema (`{ loopLimitByMode: { normal, premium, reasoning } }`, all nullable integers) referenced from `AdminPlanState` / `AdminPlanInputBase`. The orval client was regenerated (`pnpm --filter @persai/contracts run generate`); the regenerated `packages/contracts/src/generated/**` is checked in and prettier-formatted.
- **Admin UI.** `apps/web/app/admin/plans/page.tsx` gains a new "Per-turn cap" input next to the existing "Daily cap" on every editable tool row in `ToolActivationsEdit` (with `setPerTurnCap` mirroring `setLimit`; blank means "use code default"), and a new `Sec` panel "Tool budgets (loop limits per execution mode)" with three integer inputs for `normal` / `premium` / `reasoning` plus help text explaining the resolution order. `ToolActivationDraft` and `PlanDraft` are extended; `planToDraft` populates from `AdminPlanState.toolBudgets.loopLimitByMode` defensively (null ŌåÆ ""), `draftToPayload` parses each blank ŌåÆ `null` and each filled value through `parseStrictIntegerDraft` so 0 / negative is caught at submit-time on the client. `apps/web/app/admin/plans/page.test.tsx` fixture seeds `perTurnCap: null` and a defaulted `toolBudgets` block.
- **Tests.** New `apps/api/test/tool-budgets-policy.test.ts` exhaustively covers default + parser + resolver paths (round-trip schema marker, all-null = no-override, partial overrides survive, strict parser rejects 0 / -1 / 1.5 / NaN / "5" / true with `BadRequestException`, lenient resolver always returns defaults for garbage and keeps valid leaves while degrading bad ones to null). `apps/api/test/manage-admin-plans.service.test.ts` extended with a Slice L1 block (`parseUpdateInput` carries `perTurnCap`; `toWriteInput` writes the `persai.toolBudgets.v1` block only when there is an override; the override is omitted when every loop limit is null; strict parser rejects non-positive `perTurnCap` and loop limits with field-path in the error; `toAdminPlanState` round-trips both fields). `apps/api/test/runtime-tool-policy.test.ts` adds `perTurnCap` to its `planToolQuotaPolicy` fixture and asserts the runtime tool policy carries it for `web_search` (= 2), keeps `null` for `image_generate`, and keeps `null` for synthetic (`compact_context`) and hidden-internal (`cron`) policies. `apps/runtime/test/runtime-bundle-registry.service.test.ts` adds two new fixtures + assertions: `perTurnCap = 0` on a tool policy is rejected (`/perTurnCap must be null, omitted, or a strictly-positive integer/`), and `toolBudgets.loopLimitByMode.normal = 0` is rejected (`/toolBudgets\.loopLimitByMode\.normal must be null or a strictly-positive integer/`).

### Files touched

- `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma` (new `perTurnCap Int?` column on `PlanCatalogToolActivation`)
- `apps/api/prisma/migrations/20260423030000_adr074_l1_plan_per_turn_cap/migration.sql` (NEW ŌĆö additive, reversible)
- `apps/api/prisma/seed.ts`, `apps/api/prisma/tool-catalog-data.ts` (carry `perTurnCap` through seeding)
- `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/tool-budgets-policy.ts` (NEW ŌĆö parser / serializer / resolver / `hasAnyToolBudgetOverride`)
- `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/admin-plan-management.types.ts` (new `AdminPlanToolBudgets`, `perTurnCap` on activations)
- `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/manage-admin-plans.service.ts` (parse/write/round-trip `toolBudgets` + `perTurnCap`)
- `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/runtime-tool-policy.ts` (`perTurnCap` on `ToolQuotaPolicyEntry` + `RuntimeToolPolicy`)
- `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/materialize-assistant-published-version.service.ts` (new `resolvePlanToolBudgets`; conditional `runtime.toolBudgets` emit)
- `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/seed-tool-catalog.service.ts` (carry `perTurnCap` through `syncToolActivations`)
- `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/domain/assistant-plan-catalog.entity.ts`, `ŌĆ”/repository.ts`, `ŌĆ”/infrastructure/persistence/prisma-assistant-plan-catalog.repository.ts` (domain + repo carry `perTurnCap`)
- `apps/runtime/src/modules/bundles/runtime-bundle-registry.service.ts` (`assertValidToolPolicy` validates `perTurnCap`; new `assertToolBudgetsConfig`)
- `packages/contracts/openapi.yaml` (new `AdminPlanToolBudgets`, `perTurnCap` on activations)
- `packages/contracts/src/generated/**` (regenerated orval client; prettier-formatted)
- `apps/web/app/admin/plans/page.tsx` (Per-turn cap input + Tool budgets panel + draft/payload wiring)
- `apps/web/app/admin/plans/page.test.tsx` (fixture updated to satisfy the new required fields)
- `apps/api/test/tool-budgets-policy.test.ts` (NEW)
- `apps/api/test/manage-admin-plans.service.test.ts` (Slice L1 block added)
- `apps/api/test/runtime-tool-policy.test.ts` (`perTurnCap` propagation assertions)
- `apps/runtime/test/runtime-bundle-registry.service.test.ts` (warm-path rejection assertions)
- `docs/CHANGELOG.md`, `docs/SESSION-HANDOFF.md`, `docs/ADR/074-humanity-and-cost-polish-program.md`

### Verification gate

- `pnpm lint` ŌĆö clean across all 14 workspace projects
- `pnpm format:check` ŌĆö clean (after re-formatting the regenerated orval output and the new files)
- `pnpm typecheck` ŌĆö clean across all 14 workspace projects (runtime-contract / runtime-bundle / runtime / api / web all compile against the new fields)
- `pnpm --filter @persai/api test` ŌĆö green
- `pnpm --filter @persai/runtime test` ŌĆö green
- `pnpm --filter @persai/web test` ŌĆö green (19 files, 92 tests)

### What is still pending

- **`persai-dev` deploy + 6 founder live UI gates.** The original 4 L1 gates from the previous entry (chitchat-short Ōēż1 tool call, tool-heavy-search completes within new limits, long-session-200 unchanged, budget exhaustion visible in trace) **plus** 2 new gates for this closeout: (5) on `/admin/plans`, edit `web_fetch` "Per-turn cap" to 7 ŌåÆ save ŌåÆ re-open ŌåÆ cap persists, and a fresh assistant turn that emits 8 sequential `web_fetch` calls substitutes `tool_budget_exhausted` with `limit: 7, observed: 8`. (6) on `/admin/plans`, set "Tool budgets / normal" to 5 ŌåÆ save ŌåÆ re-open ŌåÆ value persists, and a fresh assistant turn in normal mode runs exactly 5 `web_fetch` iterations before substitution (visible via WARN line `[tool-budget-exhausted] reason=loop_limit limit=5 observed=6`).
- **No backfill migration** on existing `plan_catalog_tool_activations` rows (legacy rows stay NULL = inherit code defaults; deploy is byte-for-byte identical until an admin actually saves a per-turn cap from the UI).

### Risks and residuals

- **Bundle JSON growth.** The compile pipeline only emits `runtime.toolBudgets` when at least one leaf is non-null and only emits `perTurnCap` per tool policy when explicitly set (NULL is omitted by Prisma ŌåÆ `RuntimeToolPolicy`). So bundles for plans that don't override anything stay the same size as before the closeout.
- **Schema marker discipline.** The `toolBudgets` JSON block carries `schema: "persai.toolBudgets.v1"`. Future schema revisions must bump this marker, and `resolveStoredPlanToolBudgets` must remain forward-compatible (degrade unknown leaves to defaults instead of throwing) so an old API can read a new bundle without crashing during a rollout window.
- **UI defensive parsing.** `draftToPayload` rejects `0` / negative / fractional via `parseStrictIntegerDraft` at submit-time, _and_ the API parser rejects them again, _and_ the warm-path validator rejects them a third time. Three layers is intentional ŌĆö each layer covers a different operator workflow (UI direct edit, API curl, manual Postgres edit).

### What is NOT in this slice

- Backfill migration on existing rows.
- R1 (model-supplied per-call budget hints) / R2 (parallel tool dispatch) / R3 (compound tools) ŌĆö still out of scope.
