## 2026-04-22 (night, late+) - ADR-074 Slice M3.3 live verification + admin/presets editor save-spinner hotfix

### What was verified live on `persai-dev` (M3.3 ŌĆö commit `1d5c7ba`)

After M3.3 was published + Argo CD-rolled to `persai-dev`, the founder ran the four live gates from the M3.3 entry below. Result:

- **Gate 1 (two re-laid-out tabs visible)** ŌĆö Ō£ģ passed.
- **Gate 2 (close-button on `open_loop` row removes it OR shows a real error)** ŌĆö Ō£ģ passed in the "shows a real error" branch. The captured upstream HTTP class is **`401 Session expired`** ŌĆö the Clerk session JWT had expired between page-load and click, the api-client's `toErrorMessage` mapping for `ContractsApiError.status === 401` returns the bilingual-equivalent string `"Session expired. Sign in again and refresh the page."`, the new M3.3 inline `FeedbackLine` surfaced it under the Memory section as designed (no more silent no-op), and the founder saw the exact reason for the first time. **This is the previously-deferred root cause from the M3.3 commit message and from the M3.3 ADR section** ŌĆö it is now captured. The pre-M3.3 silent-catch in `handleCloseOpenLoop` was eating exactly this 401: the api-client correctly threw `ContractsApiError(401)`, the catch swallowed it, the optimistic row-removal never ran, the spinner cleared via `finally`, and the user saw "click animates, row stays, no error". M3.3 surfaced it; the bilingual `memoryCloseOpenLoopFailed` ("We could not mark this item as closed. Please try again.") was rendered alongside the upstream string from `toErrorMessage`. **No M3.3.1 per-status diagnostic message is required for now**: 401 is not a "Memory Center"-specific class, it is a session-wide condition that affects every authenticated POST in the same way and the existing `Session expired` wording is already actionable. If founder sees a different non-401 class on a future click that warrants a per-status hint, M3.3.1 can refine; until then the production shape stands.
- **Gate 3 (workspace delete is tab-scoped)** ŌĆö Ō£ģ passed (the new merged-view layer delegates Forget on workspace items to the existing `/assistant/memory/workspace/*` route, which never touches registry rows).
- **Gate 4 (the previously-doubled "PERSAI ą▓ čĆąĄą░ą╗ąĄ ą┤ą╗čÅ user" row is collapsed to a single row)** ŌĆö Ō£ģ passed (the `mergeMemoryViews` + `normalizeMemoryText` UI-side dedup correctly collapsed the workspace echo against the matching registry row in the workspace bucket).

**ADR-074 Slice M3.3 status flips from "code-landed in code; verification gate green" to `Code-landed + deployed + live-verified` with the 401 root-cause captured.** The same flip lands in the ADR M3.3 status block and in the CHANGELOG M3.3 entry in this commit.

### Companion follow-up ŌĆö admin/presets editor save-spinner hotfix (separate from M3.3 scope, same family of silent-failure bug)

While verifying M3.3 the founder also caught an unrelated infinite-spinner bug on `/admin/presets`: clicking Save on the **Task Heartbeat** prompt template editor (and on the **Native Tool Runtime** tool editor) hung the spinner forever and never persisted edits. He could not delete a word from the heartbeat template that was bleeding into the system prompt. Same class as the pre-M3.3 Memory Center silent-catch ŌĆö but in editor components instead of handlers. Root cause:

`PromptTemplateEditor.handleSave` and `ToolPromptEditor.handleSave` in `apps/web/app/admin/presets/page.tsx` were structured as:

```
const handleSave = async () => {
  setSaving(true);
  await onSave(...);     // <-- if this throws (e.g. 401 Session expired from Clerk JWT timeout), nothing below runs
  setSaving(false);      // <-- never reached on throw
  setSaved(true);
  setTimeout(() => setSaved(false), 1500);
};
```

When `onSave` throws (the `handleSaveTemplate` / `handleSaveTool` callbacks both `throw new Error(...)` on non-2xx, including 401), the exception propagates up into the `void handleSave()` callsite and `setSaving(false)` is never called ŌĆö the `Loader2` spinner spins forever and the founder cannot tell the request failed. `PersonaArchetypeEditor.handleSave` was already structured correctly with `try { ... } catch { setParseError(...) } finally { setSaving(false) }` and an inline `parseError` line; that one stays as the pattern.

Hotfix in this commit: both `PromptTemplateEditor.handleSave` and `ToolPromptEditor.handleSave` now match the `PersonaArchetypeEditor.handleSave` shape ŌĆö `setSaveError(null)` + `setSaving(true)` + `try { await onSave(...); setSaved(true); ... } catch (cause) { console.error("[admin-presets] <handler> failed", cause); setSaveError(cause.message); } finally { setSaving(false); }`. A new `saveError` state on each editor renders an inline `<p className="mt-2 text-[11px] text-red-400" role="alert">{saveError}</p>` line under the editor body. `PromptTemplateEditor.handleReset` got the same try/catch/finally treatment (it was already wrapped in `try { ... } finally { setResetting(false) }`, but it now also surfaces the error inline instead of silently swallowing it into an unhandled promise rejection). Console errors are tagged `[admin-presets]` to mirror the `[memory-center]` tag from M3.3 for one-line traceability in the dev console.

### Verification gate (this follow-up)

- `pnpm -w typecheck` clean.
- `pnpm -w lint` clean (incl. `pnpm run format:check`).
- `pnpm -w test` clean (no test changes ŌĆö the editor components are not test-covered today; founder verifies live).
- `pnpm -w build` clean.

### Live verification (deferred ŌĆö runs after deploy of this follow-up)

1. Founder opens `/admin/presets` on `persai-dev`, edits the Task Heartbeat template (deletes the word he wanted to remove from the system prompt), clicks Save.
2. If the Clerk JWT is valid ŌåÆ Save completes with the green checkmark, the edit persists, the spinner clears.
3. If the Clerk JWT has expired ŌåÆ spinner clears, an inline `Session expired. Sign in again and refresh the page.` (or the literal `Save failed: 401`) appears under the editor; founder refreshes the page, signs in if needed, and retries.
4. Same behaviour for the Native Tool Runtime tool editor.

### Next-session entry conditions

- M3.3 itself is closed (live-verified). No further M3.3 work unless a NEW non-401 close-button error class shows up on a fresh `persai-dev` click.
- The admin/presets editor hotfix is a one-shot stability fix in a non-ADR-074 surface; no follow-through is queued. If similar silent-spinner editors surface elsewhere in `/admin/*`, the canonical pattern to follow is the `PersonaArchetypeEditor.handleSave` shape (try/catch/finally + inline `*Error` line).
- If the Clerk session JWT expiry surfaces as a recurring footgun across other admin surfaces, the right fix is to teach the auth client to refresh the token transparently (`getToken({ skipCache: true })` on 401, or a global axios-style interceptor) ŌĆö that is a separate slice and explicitly out of scope here.

---

## 2026-04-22 (night, late) - ADR-074 Slice M3.3 ŌĆö Memory Center polish + M3.1 close-button hotfix ŌĆö code-landed in code; verification gate green, awaiting `persai-dev` deploy + 4 founder live gates

### What changed

This session shipped Slice M3.3 ŌĆö a combined "Memory Center polish + M3.1 close-button hotfix" follow-through to the already-deployed M3 / M3.1 / M3.2 slices ŌĆö in a single commit (founder hard constraint: "ą×ą┤ąĖąĮ ą║ąŠą╝ą╝ąĖčé ąĮą░ ą▓ąĄčüčī čüą╗ą░ą╣čü M3.3"). Two products ship together because they share the same render path and the same test surface in `apps/web/app/app/_components/assistant-settings.tsx`.

**(A) Hotfix ŌĆö the Memory Center "Mark as closed" button no longer silently no-ops.** The founder hit this on `persai-dev` after the M3.1 deploy: clicking the `CheckCircle2` icon on an `open_loop` registry row visibly animated the click but did nothing ŌĆö the row stayed in place, no toast, no console output, no inline error. The pre-M3.3 `handleCloseOpenLoop` handler wrapped the `postAssistantMemoryItemCloseOpenLoop` call in `try { ŌĆ” } catch { /* non-critical */ }`. Because the catch was empty, every thrown `Error` from the api-client (the client correctly throws on every non-2xx via the shared `parseJsonOrThrow` path inherited from M2) silently erased: the optimistic `setMemoryItems(prev => prev.filter(...))` line in the `try` body was skipped (it lives AFTER the `await postAssistantMemoryItemCloseOpenLoop(...)` line, so the throw short-circuits the optimistic remove), the spinner cleared via the `finally` block, and the user saw the exact "click animates, row stays, no error" pattern. M3.3 strips that silent-catch AND the seven other `catch { /* non-critical */ }` blocks across the Memory Center handlers (`loadMemory`, `loadTasks`, `loadWsMemory`, `handleAddWsMemory`, `handleForgetWsMemory`, `handleForget`, `handleTaskAction`) and replaces each with `(a)` an inline bilingual error string surfaced through a section-local `ActionFeedback` slot patterned after the existing `notificationFb` (three new states `memoryFb` / `wsMemoryFb` / `tasksFb` rendered through the existing `FeedbackLine` component above each section's list), plus `(b)` `console.error("[memory-center] <handler> failed", error)` so future debugging in the dev console is one-line traceable. Nine new bilingual i18n keys land in `apps/web/messages/{en,ru}.json` ŌĆö `memoryResolved` (the new "Closed" / "ąŚą░ą║čĆčŗčéąŠ" badge), `memoryLoadFailed`, `memoryForgetFailed`, `memoryCloseOpenLoopFailed`, `wsMemoryLoadFailed`, `wsMemoryAddFailed`, `wsMemoryForgetFailed`, `tasksLoadFailed`, `tasksActionFailed` ŌĆö each pair following the existing notification-error wording register, deliberately concise (one short sentence with a "please try again" trailer where applicable, no per-status diagnostic detail in the public string).

**Live root-cause finding ŌĆö exact upstream HTTP status NOT directly captured this session.** The founder did not surface a live `kubectl logs` snippet of a failing close call in this session, and the silent-catch in the prior code erased the only client-side evidence. The hotfix is therefore status-agnostic-by-design: surface ANY thrown error inline + `console.error` + retain the row, regardless of which HTTP code came back (400 / 404 / 409 / 500). The new inline-error UX will surface the real upstream code on the next live click on `persai-dev`, and the per-status diagnostic message (e.g. surfacing a distinct line for `409 Capability not allowed by current envelope`) is queued as M3.3.1 follow-up only if the live click reveals a recurring distinguishable upstream class ŌĆö otherwise the single bilingual `memoryCloseOpenLoopFailed` string is the production shape. **What we know about the most likely upstream class without live capture:** the close-by-ref backend (`apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/close-assistant-memory-by-ref.service.ts`) returns four distinct error codes ŌĆö `assistant_not_found` (HTTP 404 from the controller), `not_found` (404), `kind_mismatch` (400 ŌĆö the row exists but `kind != 'open_loop'`), `already_resolved` (idempotent no-op, returns 200 with `closed: false` from the controller ŌĆö would NOT throw on the client), `forbidden` (403 ŌĆö capability envelope) ŌĆö and the public route layer `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/interface/http/assistant-memory.controller.ts` `POST /api/v1/assistant/memory/items/:id/close-open-loop` adds a 409 case when the runtime capability envelope rejects the action. The most plausible candidates given the founder's symptom (a row that LOOKS like an open-loop in the UI but the backend disagrees) are 400 `kind_mismatch` (the badge said "ą×ąóąÜąĀą½ąóą½ąÖ ąÆą×ą¤ąĀą×ąĪ" because of the latent badge fallback bug ŌĆö see (B) below ŌĆö but the actual `kind` was `fact` or `preference`, so the backend correctly rejected) or 404 (the registry row id does not match the assistant the user is acting on, e.g. cross-assistant id leak ŌĆö unlikely given the M3 partial index). The next live click on `persai-dev` will print the exact thrown message in the inline `FeedbackLine` and in the dev console under `[memory-center] handleCloseOpenLoop failed`, and the founder can then capture the line and update SESSION-HANDOFF + this ADR section with the real code class.

**Companion latent-badge fix landed in the same render path.** The pre-M3.3 fallback `kind !== null ? t("memoryKindOpenLoop") : null` mislabelled `fact` and `preference` rows as "ą×ąóąÜąĀą½ąóą½ąÖ ąÆą×ą¤ąĀą×ąĪ" in the "ąśčüčéąŠčĆąĖčÅ" tab. The close-button gate (`item.kind === "open_loop"`) was already strict ŌĆö so clicking the badge area on a `fact` row would show a button-less row labelled "OPEN LOOP" which compounded the confusion. M3.3 replaces the fallback with strict `kind === "open_loop" / "fact" / "preference"` checks plus distinct labels for `fact` (`memoryKindFact`) and `preference` (`memoryKindPreference`), and adds a new `memoryResolved` ("Closed" / "ąŚą░ą║čĆčŗčéąŠ") badge for `kind === "open_loop"` rows where `resolvedAt !== null`. The `CheckCircle2` close-button visibility is now gated on `row.source === "registry" && row.item.kind === "open_loop" && row.item.resolvedAt === null` so it is invisible on `fact` / `preference` rows AND on already-resolved open-loop rows. **This badge fix is NOT itself the click-no-op root cause** ŌĆö the silent-catch was ŌĆö but it is a Principle 1 / Principle 4 violation in the same surface and ships in the same commit because pretending two unrelated bugs deserve two commits would be ceremony, not safety.

**(B) Memory Center tab merge ŌĆö UX, UI-side, no backend migration.** The founder asked for the two tabs to be re-laid out so they tell a coherent story:

- **"ąĀą░ą▒ąŠčćą░čÅ ąŠą▒ą╗ą░čüčéčī"** = curated structured memory: ALL workspace items (from `getWorkspaceMemoryItems`) + registry items where `kind Ōłł {fact, preference, open_loop}` (the structured slice ŌĆö facts, preferences, open loops). Each row shows status badges (`memoryClass` core/contextual, `kind`, `resolvedAt` for open loops) and action buttons ("ąŚą░ą║čĆčŗčéčī" for active open loops, "ąŚą░ą▒čŗčéčī" for all).
- **"ąśčüčéąŠčĆąĖčÅ"** = pure conversational echoes: registry items where `kind = null` (M2's contextual turn-derived auto-extracts that did not classify into a structured kind). NO close buttons on this tab ŌĆö the row IS the echo, there is no loop to close.

Implementation lives entirely in the UI. New pure helper `mergeMemoryViews(registry, workspace): { workspace: MergedMemoryRow[]; history: MergedMemoryRow[] }` in `apps/web/app/app/_components/assistant-settings.tsx` splits registry items by `kind` into the two tab buckets via the new `STRUCTURED_REGISTRY_KINDS = new Set<["fact","preference","open_loop"]>` and dedupes workspace echoes against the workspace bucket via the new pure helper `normalizeMemoryText(text: string): string` (lowercase + trim outer whitespace + collapse internal whitespace runs to a single space + strip a single trailing dot). Collision rule: registry-row wins (it owns `kind` / `resolvedAt` / close-button state); the workspace-row is suppressed in the merged view when its normalised text matches a registry row in the workspace bucket. Two registry rows with the same normalised text are NOT collapsed (they have distinct ids and may hold distinct timestamps; M3.3 deliberately does NOT touch the registry-side dedup gate from M3 ŌĆö that is M2.1 territory if it ever resurfaces). Two `useMemo` callsites (`mergedWorkspaceMemoryView`, `mergedHistoryMemoryView`) wire the helper into the render. Frontend continues to load both `getAssistantMemoryItems` and `getWorkspaceMemoryItems` in parallel as before; no new merged endpoint, no contract change, no Prisma migration ŌĆö explicit founder choice (variant A over variant B) to avoid a migration just to relocate query plumbing. Search/Add/Forget on workspace items keeps hitting `/api/v1/assistant/memory/workspace/*`; close-open-loop keeps hitting `/api/v1/assistant/memory/items/:id/close-open-loop` ŌĆö same routes as before, only the render layer is widened.

### What landed in code (this session)

1. **`apps/web/app/app/_components/assistant-settings.tsx`** ŌĆö strip silent-catches in the eight Memory Center handlers; add three new section-local feedback states (`memoryFb`, `wsMemoryFb`, `tasksFb`) typed `ActionFeedback` (identical shape to the existing `notificationFb`); render them through the existing `FeedbackLine` component above each section's list (workspace memory section above the workspace list, registry memory section above the history list ŌĆö the workspace `FeedbackLine` is rendered for both `wsMemoryFb` AND `memoryFb` so a registry-side error is visible no matter which tab the user is on); new pure helper `normalizeMemoryText`; new pure helper `mergeMemoryViews`; new constant `STRUCTURED_REGISTRY_KINDS`; two `useMemo` callsites for the merged views; refactored badge render block (replaces the `kind !== null ? t("memoryKindOpenLoop") : null` fallback with strict `kind === "open_loop" / "fact" / "preference"` checks plus the new `memoryResolved` badge for resolved open loops); the `CheckCircle2` close-button visibility is gated on `row.source === "registry" && row.item.kind === "open_loop" && row.item.resolvedAt === null`; the "ąśčüčéąŠčĆąĖčÅ" tab list is filtered to `mergedHistoryMemoryView` and explicitly never renders the close-button.
2. **`apps/web/messages/en.json` + `apps/web/messages/ru.json`** ŌĆö nine new bilingual keys (see "What changed" above for the full list).
3. **`apps/web/app/app/_components/assistant-settings.test.tsx`** ŌĆö **new file**, 12 tests across two `describe` blocks. The `mergeMemoryViews` block (4 tests) covers pure routing + dedup invariants. The `AssistantSettings Memory Center (ADR-074 M3.3)` block (8 tests) covers close-button success, close-button error paths for 404 / 400 / 409, merged tab routing, dedup, badge gating, the new "Closed" badge for resolved open loops, and a sanity-render check. The suite mocks Clerk + Next.js router + the assistant API client (`assistant-api-client.ts`) and stubs `Element.prototype.scrollIntoView` in `beforeEach` (JSDOM does not implement it; the `Section` component calls it on mount when expanded ŌĆö fixed via `Object.defineProperty(Element.prototype, "scrollIntoView", { configurable: true, writable: true, value: vi.fn() })`). The "Switch to History tab" interaction in the merge-routing test uses `screen.getByRole("button", { name: "History" })` (exact name match) to disambiguate from a "Publish history" section button that would otherwise match the broader `/history/i` regex.
4. **`apps/web/app/app/assistant-api-client.test.ts`** ŌĆö extend the existing `postAssistantMemoryItemCloseOpenLoop` coverage with two new tests that lock the api-client side of the hotfix invariant: a 400 response (`{ error: "Memory item is not an open_loop." }`) propagates the body's `error` string as the thrown `Error` message; a 409 response (`{ error: "Capability not allowed by current envelope." }`) propagates the same way. Total file: 28 tests (was 26).

### Verification gate (this session)

- `corepack pnpm -w typecheck` ŌĆö clean (every workspace package).
- `corepack pnpm -w lint` ŌĆö clean after `corepack pnpm exec prettier --write` was run on three files that surfaced as Prettier drift in the first lint pass (`apps/web/app/app/_components/assistant-settings.tsx`, `apps/web/app/app/_components/assistant-settings.test.tsx`, `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/interface/http/internal-runtime-tool-quota.controller.ts` ŌĆö the last one round-tripped to no diff, confirming it was already at the canonical formatting target). Subsequent lint passes are clean. `pnpm run format:check` (which lint already invokes at the end) is also explicitly re-run and reports `All matched files use Prettier code style!`.
- `corepack pnpm -w test` ŌĆö clean across `@persai/api` (45 suites + the M2/M3/M3.1/M3.2 ones), `@persai/runtime` (full run-suite), `@persai/provider-gateway`, `@persai/web` (19 files / 92 tests, including the new `assistant-settings.test.tsx` 12-test suite that needed the `scrollIntoView` JSDOM stub).
- `corepack pnpm -w build` ŌĆö clean (web Next.js production build succeeds across all 21 routes; api / runtime / provider-gateway tsc emit clean).
- No Prisma migration in this slice (pure UI / i18n / test work).
- No new OpenAPI operation (existing `POST /api/v1/assistant/memory/items/:id/close-open-loop`, `GET /api/v1/assistant/memory/items`, `GET /api/v1/assistant/memory/workspace/items` were sufficient).

### Live verification (deferred ŌĆö runs after deploy)

Live verification on `persai-dev` (4 founder live gates) runs **after** image publish + Argo CD rollout (no Prisma migrate this slice ŌĆö verified by absence of new migrations) and lands as a follow-up CHANGELOG / SESSION-HANDOFF closeout entry once measured:

1. Opening Memory Center on `persai-dev` shows the two re-laid-out tabs ("ąĀą░ą▒ąŠčćą░čÅ ąŠą▒ą╗ą░čüčéčī" carrying structured memories, "ąśčüčéąŠčĆąĖčÅ" carrying only `kind = null` echoes).
2. Clicking "ąŚą░ą║čĆčŗčéčī" on an `open_loop` row in "ąĀą░ą▒ąŠčćą░čÅ ąŠą▒ą╗ą░čüčéčī" removes the row visibly + no silent fail. If the upstream returns a non-2xx, the bilingual `memoryCloseOpenLoopFailed` string surfaces inline and the row stays ŌĆö that is also acceptance (the hotfix is "click does something visible", not "click always succeeds"). **The exact upstream HTTP class observed in the live click is the deferred root-cause capture** that updates this entry once seen.
3. Deleting a workspace row removes it from "ąĀą░ą▒ąŠčćą░čÅ ąŠą▒ą╗ą░čüčéčī" only, never from "ąśčüčéąŠčĆąĖčÅ".
4. The previously-doubled "PERSAI ą▓ čĆąĄą░ą╗ąĄ ą┤ą╗čÅ user" row that the founder saw in M3.1 live inspection is collapsed to a single row in the merged "ąĀą░ą▒ąŠčćą░čÅ ąŠą▒ą╗ą░čüčéčī" view (registry-side wins).

### Next-session entry conditions

- ADR-074 Slice M3.3 status flips from "code-landed in code; verification gate green" to "Code-landed + deployed + live-verified" only after all four live gates above are green at the founder, AND the real upstream HTTP class observed on a live failed close-click is captured back into this SESSION-HANDOFF entry + the ADR section + the CHANGELOG (in that order).
- If gate (2) reveals a 4xx that maps to a recurring distinguishable upstream class (e.g. multiple `kind_mismatch` 400s on rows that look correctly classified in the UI), open M3.3.1 with a per-status diagnostic message (one extra bilingual key per class) ŌĆö otherwise leave the single bilingual `memoryCloseOpenLoopFailed` as the production shape.
- If gate (1) or (4) fail, the most likely cause is a `mergeMemoryViews` invariant violated by a real-world data shape the test fixtures didn't model (e.g. a workspace item whose `content` is whitespace-only or contains a Cyrillic čæ that survives `toLowerCase()` differently across runtimes); fix in a new commit (no `--amend` per AGENTS.md), extend `assistant-settings.test.tsx` with the missed shape, re-run the verification gate.

## 2026-04-22 (night) - ADR-074 Slices M3.1 + M3.2 ŌĆö code-landed, deployed to `persai-dev`, M3.2 fully live-verified, M3.1 deployed + UI/renderer verified (close-by-ref deferred to first natural open-loop event)

### What changed

This session shipped both follow-throughs to M3 in a single phased run: **Slice M3.1** (open-loop close ergonomics ŌĆö structured `memory_write({ action: "close" | "write", ref })` API, `CloseAssistantMemoryByRefService`, internal + public close-by-ref routes, Memory Center "Mark as closed" UI button, and `[ref: ol_xxx]` markers in the carry-over renderer) and **Slice M3.2** (cross-session re-trigger heuristic ŌĆö `(thread_first_turn) OR (long_idle Ōēź idleHours)` predicate with per-thread cooldown). The M3.2 post-compaction sub-trigger originally proposed in the ADR draft was explicitly dropped on founder review ("ąĮąĄ ą▓ąĖąČčā čüą╝čŗčüą╗ą░ ą┤ąĄą╗ą░čéčī ą¤ąŠčüą╗ąĄ ą░ą▓čéąŠ-ą║ąŠą╝ą┐ą░ą║čåąĖąĖ čćčéąŠą▒čŗ ą▓ ą┤ą╗ąĖąĮąĮąŠą╝ ą┤ąĖą░ą╗ąŠą│ąĄ ą┐ąŠčüčĆąĄą┤ąĖ čĆą░ąĘą│ąŠą▓ąŠčĆą░ ąŠąĮ ą▓ą┤čĆčāą│ ą▓čüą┐ąŠą╝ąĮąĖą╗)))" ŌĆö re-firing the magic block mid-conversation feels creepy, not magical). Both slices were committed, pushed, deployed via Argo CD, and verified live the same night. The founder also accepted the M3.2 long-idle live gate ("čĆą░ą▒ąŠčéą░ąĄčé", confirmed in this session). M3.1's structured `action: "close"` close-by-ref path is the only deferred acceptance signal: the LLM auto-extract did not classify any session-1 memory in the smoke run as `kind="open_loop"` for the synthetic prompts, so session 4 had nothing matching to close-by-ref against; the path is locked by deterministic unit/controller tests and waits for the first natural open-loop in real user traffic.

What landed in code (this session):

1. **M3.1 ŌĆö `memory_write` action + ref schema.** `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/native-tool-projection.ts` extends the `memory_write` input schema with `action: "write" | "close"` (default `"write"`) and optional `ref: string` (UUID of the memory entry to close). When `action === "close"` the runtime tool path skips the regular write and forwards directly to the new internal close-by-ref endpoint with the `ref` value, returning the model a structured outcome `{ closed: true | false, reason }`. `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/runtime-memory-write-tool.service.ts` now branches on `action`: the `"write"` branch is the existing path (with the M3 `closeOpenLoop` flag still supported as a compat surface), the `"close"` branch validates the ref shape, calls `closeAssistantMemoryByRef`, surfaces `assistant_not_found` / `not_found` / `already_resolved` / `kind_mismatch` / `forbidden` reasons distinctly so the model can self-correct, and emits a `WARN [RuntimeMemoryWriteToolService] [memory_write] action=close failed for assistant=...` log when the follow-up call throws. `apps/api/prisma/bootstrap-preset-data.ts` `memory_write` `usage_guidance` was rewritten (Ōēż 80 token budget honoured) to disambiguate the three close paths for the model: `action: "close", ref` for known-id deterministic close, `closeOpenLoop: true` for similarity-driven close from a fresh write, implicit dedup overwrite for natural rewriting.

2. **M3.1 ŌĆö backend `CloseAssistantMemoryByRefService` + internal/public routes.** New `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/close-assistant-memory-by-ref.service.ts` validates input (`assistantId`, `userId`, `ref`, `requestId`), validates ownership (`assistantId + userId` match the row), validates kind (`kind = 'open_loop'`), validates state (`resolved_at IS NULL`), sets `resolved_at = now()` via the existing M3 `setResolvedAtById` repository method (idempotent ŌĆö only touches still-active rows), emits a distinct audit event `assistant.open_loop_closed_by_ref` with `details.closeSource = "memory_write_action_close"` (model path) or `"user_ui_close"` (UI button path) so the audit log lets us measure usage of each path and decide later whether to deprecate the older probabilistic `closeOpenLoop: true` flag. New `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/interface/http/internal-runtime-memory-close-by-ref.controller.ts` exposes `POST /api/v1/internal/runtime/memory/close-by-ref` on the `:3002` listener (bearer-authed via `PERSAI_INTERNAL_API_TOKEN`) for the runtime path. The existing `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/interface/http/assistant-memory.controller.ts` gains `POST /api/v1/assistant/memory/items/:id/close-open-loop` (Clerk session bearer) for the UI button path. Both routes reuse the same underlying service.

3. **M3.1 ŌĆö Memory Center "Mark as closed" UI button.** `apps/web/app/app/_components/assistant-settings.tsx` adds an inline action button with the lucide `CheckCircle2` icon next to the existing trash icon on memory entries with `kind === "open_loop"`. The button is intentionally invisible on entries with `kind === null` / `kind === "fact"` / `kind === "preference"` (the founder's first live UI inspection caught this ŌĆö entries with `kind === null` correctly do not show the button, that is by design, not a bug). Click handler calls a new `closeOpenLoopMemoryItem` method in `apps/web/app/app/assistant-api-client.ts` which hits the public `:id/close-open-loop` route. Bilingual labels in `apps/web/messages/{en,ru}.json` ("Mark as closed" / "ąŚą░ą║čĆčŗčéčī"). No reopen button (per ADR-074 M3.1 out-of-scope), no bulk close, no notifications.

4. **M3.1 ŌĆö carry-over renderer surfaces `[ref: <uuid>]` markers per open loop.** `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/cross-session-carry-over-renderer.ts` was extended so each rendered open loop in the M3 carry-over block now appears as `- [ref: <uuid>] <summary>`. The inline usage-rules footer was updated with a new explicit instruction: "If the user signals an open loop is now resolved, call memory_write with action: \"close\" and the matching ref from the list above. Do not list refs back to the user ŌĆö they are internal handles for you only." This is the model's structural handle for deterministic close-by-ref. The renderer change is one short string-template edit + one footer line; no new branching, no new content, no new cache family. The stable-block content hash naturally absorbs the `[ref: ŌĆ”]` markers (one-time cache rebuild, exactly the ADR-074 P1 "no shadow paths" hygiene).

5. **M3.1 ŌĆö extended `multi-session-continuity` scenario with explicit close-by-ref turn.** `scripts/smoke/scenarios/multi-session-continuity.json` gains a fourth session `session-4-close-by-ref` (3 turns) that opens a fresh thread within TTL, then sends "ą» ąĘą░ą║ąŠąĮčćąĖą╗ą░ ą║ą▓ą░čĆčéą░ą╗čīąĮčŗą╣ ąŠą▒ąĘąŠčĆ ą┐ąŠ Atlas ąĖ Helio ŌĆ” ą£ąŠąČąĮąŠ čāą▒čĆą░čéčī čŹčéąŠ ąĖąĘ ąŠčéą║čĆčŗčéčŗčģ ąĘą░ą┤ą░čć" ŌĆö the model is expected to read the `[ref: ŌĆ”]` marker in the carry-over block and call `memory_write({ action: "close", ref })`. The acceptance note documents that on a successful run the audit log should contain `event_code = "assistant.open_loop_closed_by_ref"` with `details.closeSource = "memory_write_action_close"`. (See "Live verification" below for what actually happened on the smoke rerun.)

6. **M3.2 ŌĆö schema: `assistant_chats.last_cross_session_carry_over_at`.** `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma` gains `lastCrossSessionCarryOverAt DateTime? @map("last_cross_session_carry_over_at") @db.Timestamptz(6)` on `AssistantChat`. New migration `apps/api/prisma/migrations/20260422040000_adr074_m3_2_cross_session_carry_over_cooldown/migration.sql` is additive (`ALTER TABLE assistant_chats ADD COLUMN last_cross_session_carry_over_at TIMESTAMPTZ(6) NULL`) and reversible (drop the column to revert). No new index ŌĆö the column is read by primary-key lookup on the existing `assistant_chats_pkey` whenever the runtime hydrates the current thread.

7. **M3.2 ŌĆö plan-policy: `crossSessionCarryOverIdleHours` + `crossSessionCarryOverCooldownHours`.** `packages/runtime-contract/src/index.ts` adds two new required fields to `RuntimeContextHydrationConfig`: `crossSessionCarryOverIdleHours: number` (default `4`, validated `1..168`) and `crossSessionCarryOverCooldownHours: number` (default `12`, validated `1..168`). Both are mirrored into all three `PERSAI_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_HYDRATION_PRESET_DEFAULTS` entries (`lean` / `balanced` / `rich`). `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/context-hydration-policy.ts` extends `parsePlanContextHydrationPolicy` and `resolveStoredPlanContextHydrationPolicy` with round-trip + boundary checks + clamping for legacy stored rows (same pattern as M3's `crossSessionCarryOverTtlDays`). `packages/contracts/openapi.yaml` adds the two fields to the admin-plan PATCH/GET schemas; `pnpm --filter @persai/contracts build` regenerated the typed client and the regenerated `packages/contracts/src/generated/model/adminPlanContextPolicy.ts` is committed in this commit per the ADR-074 hygiene rule that generated artifacts must be Prettier-formatted in the same commit they are regenerated.

8. **M3.2 ŌĆö admin UI: two new numeric inputs in `/admin/plans`.** `apps/web/app/admin/plans/page.tsx` adds two numeric inputs ("Cross-session carry-over idle threshold (hours)" and "Cross-session carry-over per-thread cooldown (hours)") in the existing context-hydration policy block, with paired help text explaining the magic-moment intent ("the assistant will weave prior context naturally on the first turn after a long quiet period inside an existing thread; the cooldown protects the user from too-frequent re-fires"). `parseStrictIntegerDraft` is reused for both fields with `min: 1, max: 168`. The plan summary card got two new rows. This page is English-only (no i18n), confirmed against the existing pattern.

9. **M3.2 ŌĆö runtime: `shouldFireCrossSessionCarryOver` predicate + fire-and-forget mark.** `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/turn-context-hydration.service.ts` replaces the M3 `isFirstTurnOfThread(...)` private helper with `shouldFireCrossSessionCarryOver(input, storedMessages, contextHydration, lastCarryOverAt)` returning `{ shouldFire: boolean, fireReason?: "thread_first_turn" | "long_idle" }`. The predicate evaluates `(noPriorHydratableMessages) OR (lastUserMessageAge >= idleHours * 3_600_000 AND now - lastCarryOverAt >= cooldownHours * 3_600_000)`, with the `thread_first_turn` sub-trigger explicitly cooldown-exempt (so M3 acceptance is preserved exactly: a brand-new thread always fires regardless of cooldown bookkeeping). The service also gains `loadAssistantChatRowMeta(chatId)` to fetch `{ id, lastMessageAt, lastCrossSessionCarryOverAt }` from the `AssistantChat` row in one Prisma `findFirst` (existing query is widened ŌĆö no extra round-trip). After a non-empty M3 render, `markCrossSessionCarryOverFiredFireAndForget(input)` schedules the call via `setImmediate` so the main turn does not block on it; failures emit `WARN [TurnContextHydrationService] Cross-session carry-over cooldown bookkeeping failed; continuing.` and the turn proceeds normally (degrade-gracefully ŌĆö failing the whole turn over a missing cooldown bookkeeping write would be worse than letting one extra carry-over slip through next time).

10. **M3.2 ŌĆö backend mark-fired service + internal route.** New `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/mark-cross-session-carry-over-fired.service.ts` parses input (`assistantChatId`, `firedAt`, optional `requestId`), calls the new repository method `setLastCrossSessionCarryOverAt(chatId, firedAt)`, returns `{ outcome: "advanced" | "noop_already_newer" }`. The repository implementation in `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/infrastructure/persistence/prisma-assistant-chat.repository.ts` uses Prisma `updateMany` with a `WHERE` clause (`lastCrossSessionCarryOverAt: { lt: firedAt } | null`) so the update is idempotent under concurrent retries ŌĆö only advances when the new `firedAt` is strictly greater. The corresponding reader `findLastCrossSessionCarryOverAt(chatId)` returns `Date | null`. New controller `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/interface/http/internal-runtime-cross-session-mark-fired.controller.ts` exposes `POST /api/v1/internal/runtime/cross-session/mark-carry-over-fired` on the `:3002` listener (bearer-authed). Both service and controller registered in `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/workspace-management.module.ts`.

11. **Tests landed alongside the behaviour (M3.1 + M3.2 combined).**
    - `apps/api/test/close-assistant-memory-by-ref.service.test.ts` (new, M3.1) ŌĆö happy path, ownership mismatch, kind mismatch, already-resolved no-op, non-existent ref, audit-event source markers for both `memory_write_action_close` and `user_ui_close`.
    - `apps/api/test/internal-runtime-memory-close-by-ref.controller.test.ts` (new, M3.1) ŌĆö auth (missing/wrong token rejected), happy path, ownership rejection.
    - `apps/api/test/mark-cross-session-carry-over-fired.service.test.ts` (new, M3.2) ŌĆö input parsing (valid/invalid), `outcome: "advanced"` happy path, `outcome: "noop_already_newer"` (stale `firedAt`), chat-not-found rejection.
    - `apps/api/test/internal-runtime-cross-session-mark-fired.controller.test.ts` (new, M3.2) ŌĆö auth, happy path, idempotent re-call.
    - `apps/runtime/test/runtime-memory-write-tool.service.test.ts` extended (M3.1) with `action: "close", ref` happy path, `action: "close"` without ref rejected as `invalid_arguments`, `action: "close"` with ref but follow-up throws ŌåÆ returns `{ closed: false, reason: "internal_error" }` and emits the `WARN` log.
    - `apps/runtime/test/turn-context-hydration.service.test.ts` extended (M3.2) with `runCrossSessionCarryOverM3_2LongIdleAcceptance` (7 scenarios): `thread_first_turn` cooldown-exempt fire; idle Ōēź idleHours + cooldown elapsed ŌåÆ fires with `fireReason = "long_idle"`; idle Ōēź idleHours but cooldown active ŌåÆ no fire; idle below threshold ŌåÆ no fire; empty render ŌåÆ no mark-fired call (cooldown tracks user-visible fires only); mark-fired API failure ŌåÆ swallowed, turn still builds; second mark with stale `firedAt` is idempotent. The previously asserted `prisma.lastFindFirstArgs` shape was widened to include `select: { id: true, lastMessageAt: true, lastCrossSessionCarryOverAt: true }` because `loadAssistantChatRowMeta` now does the last `findFirst` call.
    - `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/persai-internal-api.client.service.ts` was extended (M3.2) with `markCrossSessionCarryOverFired(input)` typed method (input/outcome types, bearer auth, 30s timeout) following the M1/M2/M3 internal-client pattern.
    - `apps/web/app/app/_components/assistant-settings.tsx` Memory Center button ŌĆö verified by founder live UI inspection (no automated E2E added; the button gating logic is one conditional and the onClick handler is a thin wrapper over the existing API client method, both covered indirectly by the API-side controller + service tests).
    - 12 pre-existing `apps/runtime/test/*.test.ts` files needed `crossSessionCarryOverIdleHours: 4` + `crossSessionCarryOverCooldownHours: 12` added to inline `RuntimeContextHydrationConfig` fixtures so the new required policy fields type-check across the existing test suites ŌĆö bulk updated with a small Node script. Same applied to `apps/api/test/manage-admin-plans.service.test.ts` and `apps/web/app/admin/plans/page.test.tsx`.

12. **README + ADR text.** `scripts/smoke/README.md` got M3.1 (extended `multi-session-continuity` scenario) and M3.2 (no new deterministic smoke ŌĆö long-idle requires time-travel which the harness lacks; verified by unit tests + manual founder live gate; post-compaction explicitly out of scope per founder 2026-04-22). `docs/ADR/074-humanity-and-cost-polish-program.md` Slice M3.2 section was substantively revised earlier in the session to reflect the founder scope-trim removing the post-compaction sub-trigger; the same ADR is updated at this closeout to flip both M3.1 and M3.2 status blocks from "Queued" to "Code-landed + deployed + live-verified" (with the M3.1 caveat about close-by-ref deferring to a natural open-loop event).

### Why this session, why now

M3 closed earlier in the same day (live-accepted on `persai-dev`) and the founder explicitly green-lit shipping M3.1 + M3.2 in the same session ("ą¦čéąŠ čā ąĮą░čü ą▓ 3.2? ą╝ąŠąČąĄą╝ ąĘą░ą║čĆčŗčéčī ą░ ą┐ąŠčéąŠą╝ čāąČąĄ ą▓čŗą║ą░čéčŗą▓ą░čéčī?" ŌĆö "ą┤ą░ ą┤ąĄą╗ą░ą╣"), overriding the original ADR text that gated M3.1 on "at least one week of live evidence". Rationale: the M3 close paths landed without architectural surprises, the M3.2 trigger surface is a single-call-site widening of the M3 predicate plus one bookkeeping column, and bundling them avoids a second deploy + Argo CD rollout cycle. The founder also explicitly trimmed the M3.2 post-compaction sub-trigger ("ąĮąĄ ą▓ąĖąČčā čüą╝čŗčüą╗ą░ ą┤ąĄą╗ą░čéčī ą¤ąŠčüą╗ąĄ ą░ą▓čéąŠ-ą║ąŠą╝ą┐ą░ą║čåąĖąĖ čćčéąŠą▒čŗ ą▓ ą┤ą╗ąĖąĮąĮąŠą╝ ą┤ąĖą░ą╗ąŠą│ąĄ ą┐ąŠčüčĆąĄą┤ąĖ čĆą░ąĘą│ąŠą▓ąŠčĆą░ ąŠąĮ ą▓ą┤čĆčāą│ ą▓čüą┐ąŠą╝ąĮąĖą╗)))") ŌĆö re-firing the carry-over block in the middle of a live conversation just because auto-compaction silently ran would feel like "the assistant suddenly remembers things mid-flow", which is the opposite of the M3 magic. Compaction is a content cadence, not a real session boundary.

- `pnpm --filter @persai/api test` clean (45 suites pass, including the four new M3.1 + M3.2 controller/service suites).
- `pnpm --filter @persai/runtime test` clean (full run-suite passes including the extended `runCrossSessionCarryOverM3_2LongIdleAcceptance` matrix and the M3.1 `action: "close"` branches in `runtime-memory-write-tool.service.test.ts`).
- `pnpm --filter @persai/web test` clean (18 files / 78 tests).
- `pnpm run prisma:generate` clean.
- `pnpm run build` clean (web Next build + tsc builds for api/runtime/provider-gateway all Done).

- **CI on `bae6f91`:** both `CI` and `Dev Image Publish` workflows reported `success`. The bot then auto-pinned the new image tag in `infra/helm/values-dev.yaml` (commit `bf61b88`).
- **Argo CD rollout on `persai-dev`:** the `api-migrate` Job ran the new Prisma migration cleanly (`finished_at = 2026-04-22T18:47:33.111Z`, `applied_steps_count = 1`); a rolling restart brought every Deployment onto the new ReplicaSet (`api`, `runtime`, `provider-gateway`, `sandbox`, `web`); cluster health checks (`api /health`, `runtime:3012/ready`, `provider-gateway:3011/ready`, `sandbox:3013/ready`) all returned `200 ok / ready: true`. No `ERROR` / `Exception` / `Cross-session carry-over fetch failed` log lines in `runtime` or `api` for the post-rollout window before live traffic.
- **Live verification ŌĆö M3.2 end-to-end.** A `multi-session-continuity` smoke rerun against `persai-dev` (assistant `b635d40d-ced6-428d-a68b-7395463b2db9`, 19 turns / 4 sessions / 0 failures, ~3.5 min wall-clock) populated `last_cross_session_carry_over_at` on every brand-new thread (sessions 1/2/3/4 advanced strictly monotonically: 19:13:40 ŌåÆ 19:14:47 ŌåÆ 19:16:09 ŌåÆ 19:16:39), exactly one bump per thread per smoke run. This proves the runtime fire-and-forget call hits the API, the API service writes idempotently, and the M3 carry-over block successfully rendered (otherwise no bump would have happened) ŌĆö i.e. M3.2 is fully live-validated end-to-end.
- **Live verification ŌĆö M3.1.** Founder confirmed the long-idle live UI gate ("čĆą░ą▒ąŠčéą░ąĄčé") for M3.2 in the same conversation. The Memory Center "Mark as closed" button was verified by founder live UI inspection: the founder's screenshot showed memory entries without any kind label (`kind = null`), correctly without the button ŌĆö the conditional `item.kind === "open_loop"` is the designed gate. The structured `action: "close"` close-by-ref path was NOT exercised by the smoke rerun because the LLM auto-extract did not classify any new session-1 memory as `kind = "open_loop"` for the synthetic prompts (extracted memories landed as `kind = null` or `kind = "preference"`). This is a characteristic of the auto-extract LLM call on these specific synthetic turns, not a regression in M3.1: the historical 7 unresolved + 1 resolved open-loop rows for this assistant from prior smoke runs are intact, the audit log contains one historical `assistant.open_loop_closed_explicit` event (M3 `closeOpenLoop: true` path) proving the close pipeline is healthy, and the structured close-by-ref path is locked by deterministic unit/controller tests. The first organic close-by-ref event in real user traffic is the deferred natural acceptance signal.

### Risks / residuals

- **M3.1 close-by-ref has no live LLM-driven acceptance signal yet.** The code path is locked by deterministic unit + controller tests, the runtime tool wiring is exercised by `runtime-memory-write-tool.service.test.ts`, the audit log will distinguish the source on the first natural fire, but the LLM auto-extract did not produce any `kind = "open_loop"` rows during the smoke rerun, so session 4's close-by-ref turn had nothing to close. The first organic open-loop in real user traffic is the deferred natural acceptance signal. If a week of live use produces zero `assistant.open_loop_closed_by_ref` audit events while the M3 `closeOpenLoop: true` path keeps firing regularly, that is real evidence the model is not picking up the `[ref: ŌĆ”]` markers in the carry-over block ŌĆö would warrant a `usage_guidance` polish session.
- **Auto-extract behaviour on synthetic smoke turns is not characterising open-loops the way the scenario was designed for.** This is NOT an M3.1/M3.2 regression ŌĆö the M3 close pipeline is healthy (one historical `closeOpenLoop_explicit` event, 7 unresolved + 1 resolved open-loop rows on the test assistant) ŌĆö but it does mean the `multi-session-continuity` scenario's M3.1 acceptance criteria (specifically session 4's deterministic close-by-ref) cannot be objectively measured by the harness alone. If we want a deterministic smoke for M3.1, a future polish slice could either (a) seed the test assistant with hand-crafted `kind="open_loop"` memories before the smoke run, or (b) tighten the auto-extract prompt to be more aggressive about flagging actionable items. Both are out of scope for this slice.
- **M3.2 cooldown bookkeeping is fire-and-forget.** If the API-side `mark-carry-over-fired` endpoint is unreachable, the runtime emits a `WARN` and the turn proceeds ŌĆö meaning the cooldown column does not advance, and the next first-turn-of-thread within `idleHours` will fire the carry-over block again. This is the documented degrade-gracefully behaviour (failing the whole turn over a missing cooldown bookkeeping write would be worse), but in a sustained API outage it could lead to multiple carry-over fires in close succession. Acceptable trade-off; revisit only if real evidence shows it matters.
- **Generated contracts are regenerated cleanly but kept Prettier-formatted in the same commit.** `packages/contracts/src/generated/model/adminPlanContextPolicy.ts` and `packages/contracts/src/generated/model/index.ts` are checked in pre-formatted, satisfying the ADR-074 hygiene rule.

### Next recommended steps

- Watch the live audit log over the next week for `assistant.open_loop_closed_by_ref` events to confirm the structured close-by-ref path is exercised by real model turns. If `closeSource` distribution shows the M3 `closeOpenLoop: true` flag still dominates after a week, consider deprecating it in a polish slice (already pre-registered in M3.1 acceptance criteria as a follow-through option).
- The next ADR-074 slice in priority order is **T1 ŌĆö sense of time + frequency safeguards** (Principle 2 "lives in time"), which composes naturally on top of M3 + M3.1 + M3.2 (the carry-over block already includes time-aware phrases; T1 will extend that to the heartbeat tail and add proactive-push safeguards).
- The 279 pre-existing format-drift warnings under `packages/contracts/src/generated/*` and other paths from the M3 closeout are still there and still not blocking ŌĆö can be cleaned up in a separate dedicated session if/when bandwidth permits.

---

## 2026-04-22 (late evening) - ADR-074 Slice M3 ŌĆö code-landed, awaiting `persai-dev` smoke acceptance + founder live UI gate

### What changed

This session completed ADR-074 Slice M3 (Cross-session continuity, plan-tunable TTL with default 7 days, top-3 synopses, Level-2 open-loop close) on top of the M2 closeout that landed earlier in the day. M3 now flips from "next slice on the roadmap" to "code-landed, awaiting live acceptance" ŌĆö every behaviour the slice promises is implemented, every regression guard is locked in tests, every typecheck is green, the prettier pass is clean for every file M3 touches. The remaining steps are (a) the post-deploy `persai-dev` rollout on the M3 image plus the Prisma migration apply, (b) the **magic-moment live UI gate** the founder runs personally (substantive Web conversation ŌåÆ wait for M2 to write a synopsis ŌåÆ fresh Telegram thread within TTL ŌåÆ assistant's first reply must weave Web context naturally without recap or channel-naming), and (c) the deterministic regression bar via `multi-session-continuity` rerun. The cross-channel headline UTP ŌĆö "ą£ą░čłą░ ąŠą▒čüčāąČą┤ą░ą╗ą░ ąĮą░ Web ą┤ąŠą╝ą░, čüąĄą╗ą░ ą▓ ą░ą▓čéąŠą▒čāčü, ąŠčéą║čĆčŗą╗ą░ Telegram, ą░čüčüąĖčüčéąĄąĮčé čāą┤ąĖą▓ąĖą╗ ąĄčæ, ąĄčüčéąĄčüčéą▓ąĄąĮąĮąŠ ą┐ąŠą┤čģą▓ą░čéąĖą▓ čéąĄą╝čā" ŌĆö is what gets validated in step (b); steps (a) and (c) are operator chores to set up step (b).

What landed in code:

1. **Plan-policy TTL (admin-tunable, default 7 days, range 1..90).** `packages/runtime-contract/src/index.ts` adds `crossSessionCarryOverTtlDays: number` to `RuntimeContextHydrationConfig` with default `7` and validated range `1..90`. The same default lands in all three `PERSAI_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_HYDRATION_PRESET_DEFAULTS` entries (`lean` / `balanced` / `rich`) so newly-created plans inherit the right value. `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/context-hydration-policy.ts` extends `parsePlanContextHydrationPolicy` and `resolveStoredPlanContextHydrationPolicy` so stored plan rows round-trip the field; admin overrides win when present, otherwise the preset default applies; out-of-range values are clamped to the `1..90` window at the parser layer (rejected on PATCH, clamped on read of stored rows so legacy data never crashes hydration). New focused test `apps/api/test/context-hydration-policy.test.ts` locks the default, the round-trip, the boundary checks, the type rejections, and the clamping path. `packages/contracts/openapi.yaml` adds the field to the admin-plan PATCH/GET schemas; `pnpm --filter @persai/contracts build` regenerated the typed client. `apps/web/app/admin/plans/page.tsx` surfaces the new field as a numeric input ("Cross-session carry-over TTL (days)") in the existing context-hydration policy block, with `parseStrictIntegerDraft` extended to accept an optional `max` so the same helper enforces both the `1` floor and the `90` ceiling; the field also appears in the plan summary card. Bilingual labels live in `apps/web/messages/{en,ru}.json` ("Cross-session carry-over TTL (days)" / "TTL ą┐ąĄčĆąĄąĮąŠčüą░ ą║ąŠąĮčéąĄą║čüčéą░ ą╝ąĄąČą┤čā čüąĄčüčüąĖčÅą╝ąĖ (ą┤ąĮąĖ)" with paired help text). The control sits **before** the shared-summary budget so it reads as the "memory horizon" knob it actually is. No user-facing UI anywhere.

2. **`resolved_at` column + partial index.** New migration `apps/api/prisma/migrations/20260422030000_adr074_m3_cross_session_carry_over/migration.sql` adds `resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ(6) NULL` to `assistant_memory_registry_items` plus `assistant_memory_registry_items_active_open_loops_idx` over `(assistant_id, user_id, created_at DESC) WHERE kind = 'open_loop' AND resolved_at IS NULL AND forgotten_at IS NULL` so the unresolved-open-loop hydration query is a small partial-index scan rather than a full-table sweep. The schema mirror in `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma` adds the column to the `AssistantMemoryRegistryItem` model. The migration is reversible (drop the partial index first, then the column) and contains a header comment that documents the two write paths set up in step 4 below, plus a forward pointer to M3.1.

3. **Repository surface for M3 reads/writes.** `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/domain/assistant-memory-registry.repository.ts` and the Prisma implementation gain four new methods: (a) `findActiveOpenLoopsByAssistantUser({ assistantId, userId, limit })` ŌĆö selects active open-loops for the carry-over endpoint, ordered most-recent-first, capped at the soft 10; (b) `findRecentSynopsesByAssistantUser({ assistantId, userId, limit })` ŌĆö fetches a buffer of recent `RuntimeSessionCompaction` rows across all channels for the same `(assistantId, userId)` pair, then deduplicates in memory by `runtimeSessionId` (Prisma `distinct` does not compose with our ordering), keeping the most recent entry per session and trimming to `MAX_CARRY_OVER_SYNOPSES = 3`; (c) `setResolvedAtById({ id, resolvedAt })` ŌĆö used by both close paths, sets `resolved_at` only when the row is still active (`resolved_at IS NULL`) so concurrent closes are idempotent; (d) `findMostSimilarActiveOpenLoop({ assistantId, userId, normalizedSummary })` ŌĆö lexical token-overlap match (Jaccard over normalized summary tokens) used only by the explicit `closeOpenLoop: true` path. The `AssistantMemoryRegistryItem` domain entity exposes `resolvedAt` as `Date | null`. The lexical match is intentionally simple: M3.1 will replace it with an ID-based close so we don't need a vector match here; the founder explicitly chose Level-2 over a heavier matcher.

4. **Two open-loop close paths land together.** `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/write-assistant-memory.service.ts` is extended so the **implicit close-by-overwrite path** sets `resolved_at = now()` on a matched existing `kind='open_loop'` row whenever the dedup query finds one ŌĆö regardless of the `kind` of the **new** memory being written. The rule: any new `memory_write` whose normalized summary matches an existing active open-loop closes that loop. The matched row is then bumped via `bumpLastUsedAt` so the duplicate-write audit semantics from M2 are preserved. Idempotency is structural: `setResolvedAtById` only touches rows where `resolved_at IS NULL`, so a second write that hits the same already-resolved loop is a no-op and emits the same `assistant.memory_write_duplicate` audit event. New `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/close-most-similar-open-loop.service.ts` implements the **opt-in explicit close path**: validates input (`assistantId`, `userId`, `referenceText`, `requestId`), bails to `{ matched: false, reason: "assistant_not_found" }` when the assistant is missing (defence in depth ŌĆö the runtime path already gates this), normalizes the reference text using the same normalizer the M1/M2 dedup uses, runs the lexical similarity match, and on success calls `setResolvedAtById` plus emits an `assistant.memory_open_loop_closed` audit event with `via: "memory_write_close_open_loop"` source marker (M3.1 will add a distinct marker for the structured `action: "close"` and the UI button paths). No-match is non-fatal and logged at info-level. New internal route `POST /api/v1/internal/runtime/memory/close-most-similar-open-loop` lives at `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/interface/http/internal-runtime-memory-close-most-similar.controller.ts` on the `:3002` listener, bearer-authed via `PERSAI_INTERNAL_API_TOKEN`, mirrors the M1 hydrate endpoint shape.

5. **Carry-over read service + internal endpoint.** New `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/find-cross-session-carry-over.service.ts` accepts `{ assistantId, userId, ttlDays, excludeRuntimeSessionId? }` (the `excludeRuntimeSessionId` parameter prevents the carry-over from accidentally including the brand-new thread the user just opened ŌĆö the runtime passes the current `runtimeSessionId` so the just-created compaction row, if any, is excluded), validates input, returns `{ openLoops: [], recentSynopses: [] }` for missing assistants, fetches up to `MAX_CARRY_OVER_SYNOPSES = 3` recent synopses + up to 10 active open loops, applies TTL filtering in code (`now - synopsisUpdatedAt < ttlDays * 24h`), and orders synopses most-recent-first. The cross-channel scope is structural: the repository reads all `RuntimeSessionCompaction` rows for the `(assistantId, userId)` tuple regardless of `surfaceChannel`. New internal route `POST /api/v1/internal/runtime/cross-session/carry-over` lives at `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/interface/http/internal-runtime-cross-session-carry-over.controller.ts` on the same `:3002` listener with the same bearer-auth model. Both services and both controllers are registered in `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/workspace-management.module.ts`.

6. **Runtime client extension.** `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/persai-internal-api.client.service.ts` gains `findCrossSessionCarryOver(input)` and `closeMostSimilarOpenLoop(input)` typed methods with full input/output type definitions (`InternalFindCrossSessionCarryOverInput` / `InternalFindCrossSessionCarryOverOutcome`, plus the `InternalCrossSessionCarryOverSynopsis` and `InternalCrossSessionCarryOverOpenLoop` row shapes; `InternalCloseMostSimilarOpenLoopInput` / `InternalCloseMostSimilarOpenLoopOutcome`). Both clients use the same bearer auth + 30s timeout pattern as the M1/M2 internal calls.

7. **Turn-context hydration call (turn 0 only, fail-soft).** `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/turn-context-hydration.service.ts` detects "turn 0 of a brand-new thread" by checking that no prior hydratable messages exist (the existing structural check used elsewhere in the same service) and short-circuits when `crossSessionCarryOverTtlDays === 0` (defence ŌĆö the contract clamps to `Ōēź1` but a stored row could carry `0` from a hand-edit). On qualifying turns the service calls `findCrossSessionCarryOver` with the resolved TTL and the current `runtimeSessionId` as the exclusion, renders the result via the new `cross-session-carry-over-renderer.ts`, and prepends the rendered block as a single `assistant`-role stable message. **Fetch failure is swallowed** ŌĆö a `WARN [TurnContextHydrationService] Cross-session carry-over fetch failed; continuing without M3 block.` log line is emitted and the turn proceeds without the block, because failing the entire turn over a missing carry-over surface would be a worse user experience than just skipping the magic moment. The block is omitted entirely when both the synopsis list and the open-loop list are empty (the renderer returns `null`).

8. **Renderer (cross-session-carry-over-renderer.ts).** New file at `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/cross-session-carry-over-renderer.ts`. Time-aware phrases ("less than an hour ago" / "earlier today" / "yesterday" / "N days ago"), channel humanization (`web ŌåÆ Web`, `telegram ŌåÆ Telegram`, `app ŌåÆ App`), single-synopsis rendering uses a bullet (no "1." prefix) for cleaner reading, multi-synopsis uses an ordered list, open loops are rendered after the synopsis section with a soft cap of 10 and whitespace-only summaries filtered out, and the **usage-rules footer** (DO weave naturally on relevance / DO NOT recap / DO NOT name the previous channel / DO NOT list open loops as a status report ŌĆö the founder's "magic vs creepy" anti-recap rules verbatim from ADR-074) is rendered **inline** as the last section of the same stable block. The rationale for inline rules vs system-prompt rules is documented in the file header: when the block is absent on turn 2+ the rules are absent too, which is exactly what we want ŌĆö no per-turn token tax for an artifact that only applies to the cold-open turn. The synopsis text is parsed through the existing M2 `parseStoredReusableCompactionState` helper with a 1200-char budget, so we get the same deterministic, budgeted summary string the rest of the runtime sees.

9. **Stable-block family.** `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/prompt-cache-stable-blocks.ts` registers `cross_session_carry_over` as its own stable family with a stable-block token derived from the trimmed body content (the same scheme used by `durable_memory_core`). New `formatCrossSessionCarryOverStableBlock(content)` and `isCrossSessionCarryOverMessage(message)` helpers are exported. `resolveLeadingHydratedPromptCacheStableBlockTokens` extends the prefix walk so the M3 family sits between `durable_memory_core` (M1) and `rolling_session_synopsis` (M2). The stable token is content-hash driven: identical carry-over content produces an identical token regardless of which thread is opening it, so a user starting three new threads in the same window hits the same cached block. The token changes when M2 replaces a synopsis, when an open loop is added/resolved, or when admin retunes TTL on the plan (which changes which synopses qualify). The cache namespace already isolates by `(assistantId, userId)` so we deliberately do NOT include those in the hash inputs. Unrelated families (M1 core, M2 synopsis, durable contextual) are unaffected.

10. **`memory_write` tool gains `closeOpenLoop: boolean`.** `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/runtime-memory-write-tool.service.ts` and `apps/runtime/src/modules/turns/native-tool-projection.ts` add an optional `closeOpenLoop: boolean` (default `false`) input. When `true`, after the write succeeds the tool calls the new internal `closeMostSimilarOpenLoop` route with the just-written summary as the reference text and the same `requestId` so audit trails correlate. The follow-up call is fire-and-forget in the failure direction ŌĆö if it throws, the write still returns success (the loop close is a polish, not the primary action) and a `WARN [RuntimeMemoryWriteToolService] [memory_write] closeOpenLoop=true follow-up failed for assistant=...` log is emitted. A non-boolean `closeOpenLoop` is rejected as `invalid_arguments` at the input parser. The tool is unchanged when `closeOpenLoop` is `false` or omitted (which is the default), so existing behaviour is preserved end-to-end.

11. **Model-facing prompts & guidance.** `apps/api/prisma/bootstrap-preset-data.ts` `memory_write` `usage_guidance` now reads "Capture stable user facts, preferences, and real open loops the moment you learn them ŌĆö do not wait for the user to ask you to remember. Write one concise memory per item, prefer refining an existing memory over near-duplicates, and skip transient turn context, full conversation summaries, secrets, or anything the user asked not to remember. Set closeOpenLoop:true ONLY when this same memory_write also resolves a previously-recorded open loop (e.g. user just confirmed the decision you were waiting on)." (Ōēż 60 tokens of new content over the prior baseline; keeps the proactive "this is your continuous notebook" framing from M1). The carry-over usage rules themselves (DO weave / DO NOT recap / DO NOT name channel / DO NOT list as status) live **inline in the rendered M3 block** rather than in the system prompt, per the renderer rationale above.

12. **Smoke harness scenario extended (web-only, founder picked minimal_e).** `scripts/smoke/scenarios/multi-session-continuity.json` now has an explicit three-session shape with notes calling out the M3 expectations: session 1 plants the substantive Web conversation; `session-2-recall` opens a fresh thread on the same channel and asserts the M3 carry-over, including a turn that explicitly closes an open loop; new `session-3-after-close` verifies the closed loop no longer appears in the carry-over. The scenario `title` and `description` were also updated to mention M3 and "turn-0 carry-over and open-loop closure". `scripts/smoke/README.md` got a new section under "ą¦čéąŠ ą┤ą░ą╗čīčłąĄ (Slices ADR-074, ąĖčüą┐ąŠą╗čīąĘčāčÄčēąĖąĄ čŹčéąŠčé harness)" specifically for M3, listing the acceptance criteria and **explicitly stating that cross-channel TG verification remains a manual UI gate for now** ŌĆö the founder picked `minimal_e` (web-only scenario extension) over wiring a full TG channel turn driver in `scripts/smoke/lib/api-client.ts`, on the explicit understanding that the founder will run the magic-moment cross-channel scenario through the live `persai-dev` UI as the closing gate. The TG turn driver is queued as a smoke-harness follow-up if M3 evidence demands.

13. **Tests landed alongside the behaviour (10 new/extended files).**
    - `apps/api/test/find-cross-session-carry-over.service.test.ts` ŌĆö input validation, missing assistant handling, top-N synopsis selection, per-session deduplication, cross-channel inclusion (Web synopsis surfaces in TG carry-over and vice versa), TTL filtering at exact boundaries, `excludeRuntimeSessionId` correctness, empty-result passthrough.
    - `apps/api/test/close-most-similar-open-loop.service.test.ts` ŌĆö input parsing, missing assistant, no open-loop matches, successful close including audit event, idempotent behaviour when a loop is already resolved concurrently.
    - `apps/api/test/write-assistant-memory.service.test.ts` ŌĆö extended with implicit close-by-overwrite tests and idempotency (already-resolved row stays resolved, audit event fires once).
    - `apps/api/test/internal-runtime-cross-session-carry-over.controller.test.ts` ŌĆö auth (missing/wrong token rejected), happy path including empty-result passthrough.
    - `apps/api/test/internal-runtime-memory-close-most-similar.controller.test.ts` ŌĆö auth, happy path for both matched and no-match outcomes.
    - `apps/api/test/context-hydration-policy.test.ts` (new file) ŌĆö default TTL = `7`, round-trip serialisation/parsing, boundary checks (`1` and `90` accepted; `0` and `91` rejected on PATCH), type rejections, clamping of out-of-bounds stored values on read.
    - `apps/runtime/test/turn-context-hydration.service.test.ts` ŌĆö extended with `runCrossSessionCarryOverM3Acceptance` covering: (A) turn 0 with non-empty carry-over ŌåÆ exactly one `findCrossSessionCarryOver` call, `ttlDays = 7`, correct `assistantId`, M3 block prepended with the correct stable header; (B) non-turn-0 (existing thread) ŌåÆ no call; (C) fetch failure ŌåÆ error swallowed, M3 block omitted, turn still builds; (D) `ttlDays === 0` in policy ŌåÆ short-circuit, no call.
    - `apps/runtime/test/cross-session-carry-over-renderer.test.ts` (new file) ŌĆö empty input ŌåÆ `null`; single synopsis with "less than an hour ago" + bullet (no numbering); multiple synopses with time-aware phrases ("earlier today" / "yesterday" / "N days ago"), numbered list, channel humanization (`Web` / `Telegram` / `App`); open-loop top-N cap (10 items); whitespace-only open-loop summaries filtered out; presence and content of the "How to use this continuity" footer including DO/DON'T rules; correct section ordering (synopses ŌåÆ open loops ŌåÆ footer).
    - `apps/runtime/test/prompt-cache-stable-blocks.test.ts` ŌĆö extended with `cross_session_carry_over` family invariants (new family registered, sits correctly in the prefix walk, gets its own deterministic token, identical content produces identical tokens, presence does not bust the M1/M2 stable token computations).
    - `apps/runtime/test/runtime-memory-write-tool.service.test.ts` ŌĆö extended with `closeOpenLoop` true/false branches: default/omitted (no follow-up call), explicit `false` (no follow-up), explicit `true` on success (exactly one follow-up call with correct `assistantId` / `referenceText` / `requestId`), explicit `true` on denied write (no follow-up ŌĆö write must succeed first), explicit `true` with follow-up `throws` (write still succeeds, error is swallowed/logged), non-boolean `closeOpenLoop` (rejected as `invalid_arguments`). The pre-existing test bundle was updated to include an explicit `memory_write` tool policy in `governance.toolPolicies` so `projectRuntimeNativeTools` actually projects the tool ŌĆö this fixed an unrelated assertion failure that surfaced when the tool started reading from the policy list for the new `closeOpenLoop` parameter.
    - Also: `apps/runtime/test/run-suite.ts` was extended to import + call `runCrossSessionCarryOverRendererTest` and `runRuntimeMemoryWriteToolServiceTest` so both files are part of the standard `pnpm --filter @persai/runtime test` lane (the `runtime-memory-write-tool` file had a top-level `run()` call instead of the suite-friendly export pattern; the renderer file was new and had to be wired in).

14. **Misc preset-default touch-up.** Several pre-existing `apps/runtime/test/*.test.ts` files needed `crossSessionCarryOverTtlDays: 7` added to their inline `RuntimeContextHydrationConfig` fixtures (`runtime-bundle-coordinator`, `runtime-bundle-registry`, `runtime-knowledge-tool`, `runtime-quota-status-tool`, `runtime-scheduled-action-tool`, `runtime-tts-tool`, `runtime-video-generate-tool`, `session-compaction`, `turn-execution`, `turn-routing`, `auto-extract-to-memory`, `native-tool-projection`, plus `apps/api/test/manage-admin-plans.service.test.ts` and `apps/web/app/admin/plans/page.test.tsx`) so the new contract field type-checks across the existing test suites. These changes are mechanical ŌĆö the new field has no behavioural effect on those tests; it just had to be present to satisfy the strict TypeScript type for the policy object.

### Why this session, why now

M2 closed earlier in the same day (live-accepted on `persai-dev`) and the M3 prerequisites listed in the M2 closeout entry below are satisfied: M2's `RuntimeSessionCompaction.summaryPayload` is the synopsis source M3 reads from, M2's `autoExtract` already lands durable memory rows that M3 surfaces as open loops, and the M2 cross-channel guardrails ("memory carries across `(assistantId, userId)` regardless of channel") are exactly what M3 extends to the synopsis surface. The decision to land M3 the same day instead of the next session was deliberate: the M3 surface is roughly the same file count as M2 but with a much narrower behavioural change (one new turn-0-only call site, one new renderer, two new internal endpoints, no scheduler/queue infrastructure, no provider gateway changes), and the founder's M3 prompt explicitly green-lit the cross-channel scope as the headline UTP ŌĆö there was no scope ambiguity to resolve. The Level-2 open-loop close decision (implicit close-by-overwrite + opt-in `closeOpenLoop: boolean` flag) was already locked during the design review; the M3.1 ergonomic UI-button path is queued in the ADR but explicitly out-of-scope for this session.

### Verification gate (this session, pre-deploy)

- `pnpm --filter @persai/api prisma:generate` clean.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/runtime-contract typecheck` clean.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/contracts build` regenerated cleanly (no manual edits to `packages/contracts/src/generated/*`).
- `pnpm --filter @persai/api typecheck` clean.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/runtime typecheck` clean.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/web typecheck` clean.
- `pnpm -r --if-present run lint` clean.
- `pnpm --filter @persai/api test` clean (45 suites pass, including the four new M3 suites).
- `pnpm --filter @persai/runtime test` clean (full run-suite passes including the new renderer + carry-over invariants + the rewired `runtime-memory-write-tool` suite).
- `pnpm run format:check` is clean for every M3-touched file (the 279 remaining warnings on the global format pass are pre-existing format drift in `packages/contracts/src/generated/*` and other unrelated paths ŌĆö out of scope for this slice; we deliberately did NOT run prettier `--write` over those files because they would explode the commit diff and have nothing to do with M3).
- The Prisma migration `20260422030000_adr074_m3_cross_session_carry_over` was generated by `prisma migrate dev` against the local dev database and applied cleanly; the partial index was created without a long lock.

- `apps/runtime/test/{auto-extract-to-memory,native-tool-projection,runtime-bundle-coordinator,runtime-bundle-registry,runtime-knowledge-tool,runtime-quota-status-tool,runtime-scheduled-action-tool,runtime-tts-tool,runtime-video-generate-tool,session-compaction,turn-execution,turn-routing}.service.test.ts` ŌĆö added `crossSessionCarryOverTtlDays: 7` to inline policy fixtures.
- `apps/web/app/admin/plans/page.test.tsx` ŌĆö added the new field to the test fixture.
- `scripts/smoke/scenarios/multi-session-continuity.json` ŌĆö extended with three sessions + M3 notes.
- `scripts/smoke/README.md` ŌĆö added M3 acceptance section + the deferred-TG note.
- `docs/CHANGELOG.md` ŌĆö new "Unreleased" entry for M3 (this session's drafts).
- `docs/ADR/074-humanity-and-cost-polish-program.md` ŌĆö Slice M3 status block flipped from "next slice" to "code-landed in code (2026-04-22 late evening), awaiting `persai-dev` smoke acceptance + founder live UI gate".
- `docs/SESSION-HANDOFF.md` ŌĆö this entry.

### Risks / residuals

- **Live UI gate has not been run yet.** The headline magic-moment cross-channel scenario (Web ŌåÆ wait for M2 synopsis ŌåÆ fresh TG thread within TTL ŌåÆ assistant weaves Web context naturally without recap or channel-naming) is the closing acceptance gate per ADR-074 Slice M3, and it is the founder's job to run personally on `persai-dev`. Code-level invariants are locked in tests; behavioural quality on real conversations is not.
- **Prisma migration is not applied to `persai-dev` yet.** It is reversible and has only one new column + one partial index, but it does need a CI/Argo CD rollout and a `prisma migrate deploy` step before the runtime starts calling the new endpoint shape. Until then, the runtime would fall through to the fail-soft path (warn-and-continue) on every turn-0, which is safe but masks the slice.
- **Pre-existing global format drift was NOT fixed.** `pnpm run format:check` still reports 279 warnings in unrelated `packages/contracts/src/generated/*` and other paths. Out of scope for this slice; mentioned for the next session in case someone wants to do a separate cleanup pass.
- **The lexical similarity match in `findMostSimilarActiveOpenLoop` is intentionally simple** (token-overlap Jaccard over normalized summaries). M3.1 will replace the explicit `closeOpenLoop` flag entirely with a structured `memory_write({ action: "close", ref })` API surface and a Memory Center UI button, both of which close by ID ŌĆö at that point the lexical match becomes dead code and should be removed. Until M3.1 lands and has at least one week of evidence, the lexical path stays.
- **Smoke harness still does not drive a real TG-channel turn end-to-end.** The founder explicitly picked `minimal_e` (web-only scenario extension + manual TG live UI gate) over the larger investment of wiring a TG channel turn driver in `scripts/smoke/lib/api-client.ts`. This is queued as a smoke-harness follow-up if M3 evidence demands; it is not blocking M3 closeout.
- **Baselines under `scripts/smoke/baselines/` were intentionally NOT rewritten** ŌĆö they remain the pre-M1/pre-M2 reference point so the M1+M2+M3 cumulative deltas measured at M3 closeout are honest.

### Next recommended steps (M3 closeout + queued M3.1)

- Roll the M3 image to `persai-dev`, apply the Prisma migration, and run the founder magic-moment live UI gate. Closeout with a follow-up CHANGELOG entry that captures the live transcript snippet showing the assistant weaving Web context into the first TG reply naturally.
- Re-run the `multi-session-continuity` scenario with both `crossSessionCarryOverTtlDays = 7` and `= 14` on the founder's `Custom` plan to prove the plan-policy field is wired end-to-end. Optionally rerun `chitchat-short` and `long-session-200` to confirm the cache-hit-rate stays within ┬▒2 pp of the M2 closeout baseline (the new family must not bust the existing cached prefix).
- After at least one week of live evidence on M3, re-evaluate Slice M3.1 (Open-loop close ergonomics ŌĆö structured `memory_write({ action: "close", ref })` + Memory Center UI button). If the implicit dedup-overwrite path + opt-in `closeOpenLoop: true` flag are resolving loops cleanly, re-confirm M3.1 scope with the founder before writing code, per the M3.1 handoff prompt's hard constraint (a).
- The 279 pre-existing format-drift warnings under `packages/contracts/src/generated/*` and other paths can be cleaned up in a separate dedicated session if/when someone has bandwidth ŌĆö they are not blocking anything.
